title: Style 1
description: For briefings to department heads and governors that need to read like a feature article.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "National Geographic-style editorial infographic spread" with a printed magazine feel: a light neutral background (white/off-white) with subtle paper grain, a strict grid and generous margins. At the top, place an elegant, large serif headline left-aligned, followed by a short deck in smaller type, and several small subheadings in small caps/sans-serif that divide the content into clear sections. The imagery should be a mix of photorealistic elements and natural-history/technical illustration: one large central hero motif (photograph or realistic painting) that functions as the "hero", complemented by 2–4 smaller inset figures (circles or rectangles with thin frames) showing magnifications, cutaways or small scene vignettes. Use thin grey annotation lines with dot anchors and discreet arrows connecting text blocks to specific details in the hero motif; keep labels short and precise and avoid thick lines. Include 1–2 clean data visualisations in classic editorial style (thin lines, small axes, discreet legend boxes), plus a small map/overview in one corner with muted colour fields and colour-coded markers. Typography should be highly readable and newspaper-like: serif headlines, body copy in small serif/sans with tight but airy leading, consistent columns, small figure numbers and captions; the colour palette should be sober with mostly black/dark-grey text and sparing accent colours (1–2 muted colours) used only to mark routes, arrows or key points. The overall impression should be a premium science spread: precise alignment, discreet separators, many small informative modules, a realistic hero motif, thin callouts and print-ready quality — no neon/HUD, no glassmorphism, and no hand-drawn sketchnote aesthetic.

title: Style 2
description: For current-affairs hooks at the top of a media-literacy or digital-citizenship lesson.
prompt: Create a "cinematic neon news thumbnail" infographic in highly polished 3D illustration style (Pixar/Disney-inspired but more realistic and glossy) with dramatic lighting, strong colour contrast and clear YouTube thumbnail composition. Environment: a futuristic control room / cybersecurity lab in the background with blurred screens, warning icons and digital data panels, all in a purple/blue tone with orange highlights; use heavy depth-of-field so the background is bokeh and focus lies on the main character in the foreground. Main subject: an expressive reporter figure in medium shot with large eyes, soft skin, detailed hair with volume and gloss, gentle rim light; holding a microphone with a simple logo plate, one hand extended toward the camera in a "stop/alert" gesture. Add a large, glowing 3D shield/icon in the background right with a neon-purple outline and a warm orange crack/energy breaking through, plus flying paper sheets / "data sheets" swirling in the air with orange light trails around them (energy rings / trajectories) for a sense of motion. Typography: at the top, a small "BREAKING:" banner in warm orange with glow and motion-blur streaks; beneath it, a massive headline in a white/purple gradient with a strong drop shadow and neon edge glow; use clear hierarchy, few words, large letters, perfect readability. Colour palette: the environment dominated by neon purple, indigo and cyan, accented with warm orange/gold light trails and sparks; use bloom, lens flare, particles and small glowing embers for drama. Style rules: high detail, clean rendering, cinematic lighting, high contrast, clear silhouettes, no flat icons or hand-drawn lines, no low-resolution textures; everything should feel like a premium 3D-animated news poster with cyberpunk glow and a strong clickbait-thumbnail feel.

title: Style 3
description: For geopolitics and modern-history lessons where a tense map sets the scene.
prompt: Create a dramatic infographic with "geopolitical war-room / military briefing" imagery: use a satellite/topographical map as a full background (sand/beige/grey sea tones), with clear relief and coastlines, slightly desaturated but with high microcontrast; overlay a futuristic HUD layer with thin cyan/teal lines, coordinate grids, small boxes/panels, minimal number rows and iconic UI markers (all semi-transparent with a faint glow); place 1–2 large location/region labels in all caps (bold condensed sans, white with a dark shadow/outline) directly on the map for instant legibility; add a large, stylised silhouette of an appropriate artefact or 3D render with cool blue rim light and soft motion blur, pointing in toward a hotspot; use strong red/orange arrows and trail lines (thick, high contrast, with light glow) showing direction/movement, plus small warning icons and "target" markers to create tension; place a human presenter as a cut-out half-body on the right edge (studio photo look, neutral clothing, soft edge lighting, subtle cyan rim light from the HUD, light shadow against the background) with a serious facial expression and gaze toward the camera; colour palette: earthy map tones + cyan/teal UI + red warning accents + white headlines, with a cinematic grade, vignette and subtle film grain; composition: full-bleed background map, focus triangulated between the jet, the red arrows and the presenter, high sharpness on main subjects and less sharpness at the periphery; no logos, no real timestamps, no platform branding, no subject-specific place names — just generic large labels and generic HUD language, and no weapons.

title: Style 4
description: For onboarding briefs and welcome handouts that feel friendly but still businesslike.
prompt: Create a "Pixar-like 3D business infographic" with warm, soft studio lighting and clear thumbnail hierarchy. Background: a smooth, lightly textured gradient in purple→violet with subtle mist/bokeh and faint out-of-focus office/workshop elements (e.g. a blurred calendar/post-it wall on the left and a construction crane / iconic workshop detail on the right), all with heavy depth-of-field. Main subject: a friendly, characterful person in stylised 3D (large expressive eyes, soft skin, high level of detail), placed in the centre/lower third, leaning forward over a table and holding up a large rolled-out blueprint; the blueprint should be bright blue with thin white "technical drawing" lines, schematic gears/icons and grids, and a couple of large 3D gears "popping" out of the blueprint (tactile effect). Foreground props: a clipboard with checklist boxes, tools (a wrench), a hard hat, small papers — all in soft plastic/metal with light speculars and rounded edges. Typography: a large headline at the top in heavy sans-serif, two lines, the first word in white and the second keyword in warm orange/gold; add a subtle drop shadow and light glow for readability against the purple background. Colour palette: dominant purple/violet background, accent colours in orange/gold (gears, highlights, headline accent) and blue (the blueprint); keep everything clean, saturated but not neon, with premium 3D rendering, soft bloom, mild film grain and clear separation between foreground/mid-ground/background. Style rules: no flat illustrations, no hand-drawn lines, no photorealism — only highly polished stylised 3D, cinematic DOF.

title: Style 5
description: For case-study openers and CPD materials grounded in the look and feel of a real school.
prompt: Create a photorealistic, documentary still of a classroom in daylight with a calm, natural mood: neutral beige walls, a large green chalkboard area with visible chalk smears and eraser marks, a window along the left side letting in soft, diffuse side light, plus a ceiling fixture and a small wall-mounted speaker above the board. The camera should be placed at student eye level with a slightly wide lens (around 28–35mm), level horizon and realistic sense of depth; the colour grading should be mild and natural with slightly muted tones and subtle film grain, like a still from a school-environment photo essay. On the chalkboard there should be a large, hand-drawn chalk illustration in an anime/manga feel (done entirely with chalk, with visible chalk strokes, layers, shading and coloured chalks) dominating the centre of the board, plus a few handwritten characters/words in white chalk on the right-hand side; no printed typefaces or digital graphics. In the foreground, a worn teacher's desk with a chalk box / boxes, several coloured chalks scattered around and a classic chalkboard eraser, all photorealistic with small scratches, dust and signs of use. Emphasise the contrast between the rough, tactile chalk texture and the soft, real classroom environment; avoid stylised illustration, 3D render, neon lighting or a perfect "studio feel" — it should feel like a genuine, everyday school photograph with authentic materials, natural light and realistic details.

title: Style 6
description: For announcements, pupil reward cards, and high-energy corridor displays children actually stop to read.
prompt: Create an infographic with an extremely clean, click-friendly "creator" aesthetic: a background in bright sun-yellow with radial sunburst rays (perfectly symmetrical, high contrast, slight gradient toward the centre), minimal noise and super-crisp edges; a cut-out person in medium shot placed on the right, well-lit and colour-corrected for warm skin tones, with a strong white outline (sticker-cutout) + soft drop shadow so the subject pops from the background; text design as "stickers": several rectangular/rounded text plates stacked diagonally in the upper left, each plate with a white or light-grey fill and a strong black/deep-grey shadow, slight perspective/tilt for dynamism; typography in ultra-heavy all caps, modern sans-serif / impact-style, highly legible from a distance, with strong colour coding (e.g. one plate in red signalling a "YouTube" feel) and the rest in black/dark-grey on a light background; clear hierarchy (3–5 words max total), generous margins, no fine print, no icons that steal focus, no logos or watermarks — maximally "clean + punchy" with hard contrast, sharp outlines and clear separation between text blocks, subject and background.

title: Style 7
description: For long-form explainers where a finance-explainer dark theme keeps a dense topic readable.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "dark-mode cinematic infographic" in premium YouTube/finance-explainer style with a deep black/dark-graphite background, a subtle circuit-board pattern in the background and soft vignette and glow effects for contrast. At the top, place a large, centred main headline in white bold sans-serif for high legibility, and directly beneath it a top section acting as a summarising header with 2–3 short text blocks and a central hero illustration (semi-3D/illustrated) on a dark gradient plate; use warm accent colours (gold/amber) for key words and headlines in the top section. Below the top section, place a clear mid-heading row as a "framework" banner with thin lines and arrows pointing down toward three main columns. The main layout should consist of three large, equally wide vertical panels side by side with clear headings at the top in accent colours (left red/orange, middle gold/amber, right cyan/teal), where each panel contains a large poster-like illustration in the middle (semi-flat with light 3D, strong lighting, cinematic colour gradients) with a road/journey motif and a destination caption at the bottom; the panels should have clean frames, soft inner shadows and high contrast against the background. Around and between the panels, include many small icon callouts in a "sticker"-like semi-3D style (rocket, brain, calendar, coin, money tree, chart, lock, shopping cart, etc.) with short labels in white text, arranged in organised blocks; use thin lines/separators to group these callouts and keep the text scannable. Typography should be modern sans-serif with clear hierarchy: large headlines, medium block titles, small microcopy lines, and certain words in accent colour; include a few simple equation/icon rows at the bottom of the panels that look like a UI-style "wealth equation" with pictograms and plus/equals signs. The colour palette should be highly polished and contrasting: black/graphite base, white for main text, red/orange for the left theme, gold/amber for the middle, cyan/teal for the right, with subtle bloom/glow around icons and illustrations but without excessive neon HUD; avoid hand-drawn sketches, vintage paper and flat pastels — the goal is an elegant, dark, dramatic, highly structured infographic with three comparative columns, cinematic illustrations, icon blocks and clear typographic guidance.

title: Style 8
description: For upper-secondary tech or innovation units presented with product-keynote polish.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "dark-mode premium tech explainer" infographic in a highly polished cinematic UI style with a deep black/dark-graphite background and soft hazy gradients, like a modern sci-fi product presentation. At the top, place a large, centred title in clean bold sans-serif in white with a slight blue-tinted accent on certain keywords (subtle colour marking), and beneath the title the entire layout should consist of 5–6 large, organically rounded "glassmorphism" panels (blobby rectangles with soft curves) that flow together in a cohesive cluster, with thin light outlines, mild inner shadow and low opacity so the background shines through. In the centre, place a hero illustration in semi-realistic 3D/CG style: a human brain connected via glowing data threads to a futuristic AI core/chip, with blue-violet luminescence, small particles and subtle bloom. Each panel should have a short heading in white sans-serif with a few words of the heading highlighted in light blue, plus 1–2 lines of body copy in light grey; text should be left-aligned with clear hierarchy and plenty of breathing room. The panels should contain occasional iconic mini-scenes in the same 3D-CG style (brain profile, chip above a brain, two profiles exchanging data, UI screen, control room) with cool blue/cyan glow and soft shadows; use consistent highlights, a metallic material feel and subtle neon lines without becoming aggressive HUD. Add soft glowing lines/streams that visually connect the panels toward the centre, plus discreet bokeh dots and light trails in the background for cinematic depth. The colour palette should be controlled and futuristic: white and light-grey text, cyan/teal and blue-violet accents, with lots of black negative space; avoid warm colours, hand-drawn style, vintage, flat vector icons and hard boxes — the goal is an elegant, dark, high-tech infographic with glass panels, 3D brain/AI imagery, blue glow and premium presentation finish.

title: Style 9
description: For edtech launches and AI-tool introductions that need to feel premium to older pupils.
prompt: Create a "galaxy neon glassmorphism" edtech infographic in premium dark-mode style with a space background: a deep dark-blue/purple gradient transitioning to black, filled with small stars, bokeh dots and shimmering particles plus soft nebula sweeps in cyan, magenta and violet. At the top, place a logo-like badge with a simple icon and a large product title in modern sans-serif (white/light grey) with a subtle gradient sheen, plus a smaller tagline beneath; in one corner, a small "By …" label can sit in a rounded pill badge. The layout should be built from several large, rounded "glass" cards/panels with a semi-transparent background, soft inner shadow, thin light outlines and strong neon accents at the edges (cyan/teal, magenta, purple, green), where each panel contains a heading in bold sans-serif and 1–2 lines of body copy in light grey; certain keywords in the headings can have a gradient colour. Between the panels, soft coloured flow arrows/paths (sweeping gradient bands) should indicate progression downward, with small star sparks around the arrows to reinforce a magical tech feel. Include a large hero illustration near the top/right: a stack of books with one open and radiating rainbow-coloured light and sparks, in semi-3D/illustrated style with glow and soft shadows, but no photorealism. The sections should have clear heading rows that function as "chapter headings" (e.g. "What is…", "Key features", "Why use…"-style) in larger text, and beneath them 3–5 feature cards in varying accent colours; each feature card should have a small icon badge on the left (flat/semi-flat with glow) and text on the right. At the very bottom, place a row of 3 smaller cards in different colour themes summarising benefits, with the same glassmorphism and neon glow. Keep typography consistently sans-serif, highly readable, with plenty of padding and even spacing; use glow/bloom in a controlled way so it feels elegant, not HUD-overdone. Avoid vintage paper, hand-drawn sketches, hard black outlines and flat corporate cards without glow; the goal is a stylish, space-inspired, neon-glass infographic with a starry background, rainbow light, rounded glass panes, gradient arrows and premium app-marketing finish.

title: Style 10
description: For upper-secondary and CPD slides on analytics, data, or digital structure.
prompt: This design vision is characterised by a modern, analytical tonality that conveys a sense of order and professional digital structure. The visual framework rests on a dark base in the shade #121212, where text in #1A1A1A and a prominent purple accent in #7C3AED create a distinctive expression. To build harmonious depth, a secondary palette is used consisting of mint green (#5D9C83), pale yellow (#FDFDBC), light purple (#EBE6FF), dark grey (#565656) and a soft pink tone (#F8D7DA). The imagery itself is defined by a so-called Bento grid, where the information is divided into modular blocks with generously rounded corners of around 24 pixels. These information cards in pastel colours contrast effectively against the dark surroundings and create a clear hierarchy. Surfaces have a smooth, digitally matte finish with hints of glass-like effects and subtle colour shifts, while the diffuse, even lighting produces gentle shadows that separate the layers. Graphically, minimalist outline icons, abstract 3D elements and elegant Bezier curves with gradient fills in the charts are used to visualise data cleanly. Typography is based on a geometric sans-serif in a modern Grotesque spirit with airy letter-spacing, where bold weight is used to emphasise metrics while labels are kept at a regular weight to guarantee optimal readability throughout the analytical interface.

title: Style 11
description: For mobile-first social posts from the school — Instagram stories or newsletter cards.
prompt: Create a vertical, mobile-friendly "galaxy neon glassmorphism" product infographic in premium dark-mode style with a space background: a deep dark-blue→purple→black gradient with stars, bokeh dots and small shimmering particles, plus soft wave forms/nebula sweeps in cyan, magenta and violet along the edges and at the bottom. At the top, place a headline in modern bold sans-serif in a light colour with a subtle gradient sheen, followed by a short tagline in smaller type. Beneath this, place a clear section heading, e.g. "What is …?" in large type with certain words in gradient (e.g. cyan→magenta), followed by 2–3 lines of body copy in light grey on a large, rounded glass panel (semi-transparent, soft inner shadow, thin neon edge). Next, a heading such as "Key features" should be followed by four large rounded feature cards in a 2x2 arrangement, where each card has its own accent colour (purple, cyan, orange, green) as edge glow, an icon badge on the left (flat/semi-flat with glow), a short bold heading and 1–2 lines of description; the cards should have clear padding, soft shadows and consistent glassmorphism with a light reflection. Add sweeping gradient arrows/paths between sections (thin neon curves) and small star sparks around the arrows to give a "magical tech" feel. Further down, a heading such as "Why use …?" should be followed by three smaller cards in a row in the same glass style and different accent colours, each with a simple icon and a short benefit caption. At the very bottom, a discreet footer line in small light text can sit on a soft wave-shaped nebula background. Keep typography consistently sans-serif with clear hierarchy (large headlines, medium card titles, small microcopy), and use controlled glow/bloom so it feels elegant and readable, not overdone neon HUD. Avoid vintage, hand-drawn sketches, photorealism and flat corporate cards without glow; the goal is a stylish space-inspired, neon-glass infographic with a starry background, rounded glass panes, gradient accent words, colour-coded feature cards and soft neon arrows guiding the flow.

title: Style 12
description: For before-and-after slides that contrast a rough concept sketch with its finished real-world outcome.
prompt: Create a clean, modern infographic with a clear “before/after” story in split screen: the left half should be a loose, architectural concept sketch in black/gray on white/light gray paper surface with visible pencil lines, quick perspective lines, simple shadow wedges, a few marking lines and “rough” building volumes (minimal detail, hand-drawn feel, light dirt/graphite texture), the right half should be a bright, photorealistic final image with the same motif feel but in a real environment: crisp daylight, natural colors, high sharpness, clear materials (plaster/glass/wood), surrounding nature and rocky terrain, subtle HDR but not excessive; keep a vertical, razor-sharp split between the halves (no gradient), and use a simple visual connection between the sides by matching perspective and placement of the main shape; top: put a large, easy-to-read headline in capital letters with bold sans-serif, white text with a faint dark shadow/outline for contrast, placed in a rounded rectangle banner (semi-transparent white or very light gray, soft corners, thin border) that spans both halves; minimalist graphic style: few elements, lots of air, strong hierarchy (headline first, then the split contrast), no extra icons, no logos, no platform timestamp; color and finish: left side monochrome and matte, right side warm and natural, slight vignette for focus, gentle sharpening on main object, professional thumbnail contrast without looking “overprocessed”.

title: Style 13
description: For physics or chemistry lessons that need a single glowing model to anchor the explanation.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "dark science classroom infographic" with a black/dark-grey background, subtle grain and a central hero illustration glowing in neon cyan: a large, abstract technical model with several elliptical orbits/curves intersecting one another and with light points as "particles", with soft glow and light trails giving the feel of energy paths. In the middle of the model, place a clear core/cluster as a small 3D-rendered group of spheres in saturated colours (red/green/white) with realistic specular highlights, so it stands out against the cyan-blue. The layout should be modular: upper left a heading in white sans-serif with a short deck in smaller text beneath; around the hero illustration, several rectangular fact boxes with rounded corners and semi-transparent dark panels, each with a coloured heading strip or accent edge (red/orange/yellow) and 2–4 lines of white body copy. Use thin white connector lines from the fact boxes to specific points on the hero illustration, with small dot anchors; include 1–2 circular inset diagrams (e.g. a small "molecules" image) with a thin frame and a short label. Add a prominent callout with a large numeral in red/orange on the right side (a "fun fact" badge) and a couple of small iconic 3D object images in the corners (e.g. a small sphere/particle render) with short captions. The colour palette should be controlled: cyan/teal glow for the main illustration, white text, and warm accent colours (red/orange/yellow) for headings and badges; avoid glassmorphism with strong blur, avoid hand-drawn sketches, and avoid excessive neon HUD — the goal is a clear, educational, dark-background infographic where a glowing central model is combined with clear colour-coded fact boxes, thin connector lines and occasional 3D insets in editorial science style.

title: Style 14
description: For short data-dashboard explainers and updates to the senior team or digitalisation lead.
prompt: Create a modern infographic with minimalist "tech update" imagery: a dark near-black background with a subtle grid and light vignette; the left third consists of a dense pattern of simple, flat human silhouette icons in dark grey (matte, low contrast) arranged in rows/columns; mark exactly one of these silhouettes as "selected" by making it white and giving it a soft cyan/blue neon glow around the outline; place a large mouse pointer/arrow (white/grey, clear shadow and slight perspective) pointing toward the marked figure to create an immediate narrative without text; the right two thirds: create a large, softly glowing blue symbol form (abstract app/play bubble) with strong neon bloom and soft inner glow, placed above or near the headline area; add a clear, large headline in clean, bold sans-serif (white) with a short, simple phrase in 2 lines, centred or right-aligned, with a slight cyan outer glow for readability against the dark background; use clear hierarchy, few elements, large shapes, plenty of negative space, sharp edges and high contrast between the focus objects (white figure + blue neon) and the background (grey icons + grid); colour palette: charcoal/dark-grey base, cyan/electric-blue accent, white highlights; finishing: crisp sharpness, controlled bloom around the neon, subtle film grain; no logos, no watermarks, no small text or timestamps, no photorealistic environmental details — just clean UI iconography and strong visual focus.

title: Style 15
description: For cyber-security, GDPR, or AI-act briefings aimed at principals and IT leads.
prompt: This design should radiate an authoritative and precise tone with a sense of high security and technical expertise. The visual framework rests on a deep-blue, almost navy-black background (#0a1520) with text in pure white (#ffffff) and a golden accent colour (#eebb55), complemented by a palette consisting of dark teal (#264653), turquoise (#2a9d8f), sky blue (#4ea8de) and muted red (#e76f51). The image style is defined by modular security fields with glowing frames, connecting flow lines and status indicators combined with minimalist iconography. Surfaces have a smooth, digitally matte finish with glass-like panels (glassmorphism) and metallic gradients on the heading fields to reinforce the technical feel. The composition follows a strict grid with a vertical hierarchy in which information flows from top to bottom through clearly segmented operational zones. The lighting creates sophisticated depth through an inner glow from the interface itself, neon glow along the edges and soft backlighting behind the central elements. Framing consists of thin, glowing outlines 1–2 pixels wide in the accent colours around containers that have a semi-transparent dark-teal fill and rounded corners with a radius of 8 pixels. The icons are flat vectors presented either free-standing or enclosed in circles. Typographically, a modern geometric sans-serif in a very heavy weight is used for main headings, while body copy is kept in a clean, readable sans-serif. For labels and emblems, a condensed sans-serif in all caps is used, where the visual hierarchy is governed by gold marking primary sections, white used for content and cyan-blue for sub-labels.

title: Style 16
description: For primary-years project briefs and reading-quest handouts pupils actually want to keep.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "comic book cover" illustration in classic American adventure-comic style with clear black outlines, clean cel-shading and a printed comic-book feel. The composition should read like a cover: at the top, a large, dramatic title in heavy comic display font with white fill and thick black outline, slight 3D / drop shadow and a secondary tagline beneath in bold all caps; place a small "Mission" tag in a corner as a small box with text. In the centre, three hero-like characters stand in full figure in a triangular "hero pose" composition (one centred and facing forward, two on the sides in varied poses), dressed in expedition/outdoor gear with backpacks, binoculars and gadgets, with clear facial expressions and strong readable silhouettes. Behind them, the background should be divided into several distinct environment sectors radiating like panel wedges or a star-shaped split-screen (e.g. four different nature biomes), separated by white, jagged "burst" forms or panel frames pointing toward the centre; use clear colour fields, distinct horizons and iconic nature details, but keep them stylised and comic-book-like. Add 1–2 speech balloons with black text in classic comic lettering and clear balloon outlines, plus small decorative elements such as splashes, speed lines or a subtle halftone texture in the shadows for a printed feel. The colour palette should be saturated but controlled (turquoise/blue, green, sand/orange, ice blue) with strong contrast against the black outlines; use flat colours with a single shadow level (cel-shading), minimal gradient, and keep everything "print ready" with sharp lines. Avoid photorealism, modern minimalist UI, neon HUD and soft watercolour; the goal is a dynamic comic-book cover with a large title, three characters, split-screen nature backgrounds, speech balloons and classic comic line art.

title: Style 17
description: For language lessons that pair vocabulary with a small manga-style narrative.
prompt: Create a page in manga/anime-inspired comic style with a clear panel layout (a grid of 6 panels: three rows where the two top rows each have two wide panels and the bottom row has two smaller panels per side), where the left side of each row looks like a black-and-white manga version and the right side a colour anime version of the same panel (mirrored composition). The line work should be clean and expressive with clear outlines, chibi/childishly proportioned figures (large eyes, soft faces, small noses), and classic manga effects such as screentone/halftone shading in the black-and-white panels and dynamic speed lines / radial "burst" backgrounds in both black-and-white and colour. Use large onomatopoeic sound words in every panel (e.g. "ZAP!", "WOOSH!", "DING!") in hand-drawn comic typography with a thick outline and slight shadow; in the colour panels, the text can have a gradient/glow matching the effect (blue electric glow, warm orange glow). The imagery should be playful and magical: exaggerated energy effects (electric arcs, sparks, glowing aura around objects), clear reaction expressions on the figures (focused look, surprise, joy) and small "kawaii" markers (sweat drop, heart bubble, small stars) in the background. The right-hand colour panel should have clear but soft anime colours with cel-shading (flat colour fields + a single shadow level), a warm background gradient (sunset tones) in one panel, a cool neon-blue background in another, and strong light effects around the magical elements; the black-and-white left side should use greyscale with screentone dots and clear white highlights. Keep backgrounds simple and scene-focused, with lightly framed panels (black panel borders, white gutter) and a clean, print-ready comic finish; avoid photorealism, 3D render, minimalist infographic, or modern UI aesthetic — it should feel like a professional manga page with parallel "before/after colorisation" panels, dynamic effects, clear storytelling and strong comic sound words.

title: Style 18
description: For dramatic PE, history, or literature moments where one bold panel carries the scene.
prompt: Tone and feel: the aesthetic should be powerful, dramatic and educational with a markedly expressive, action-packed character. Visual Identity: Primary background: #FFFFFF (pure white). Primary text colour: #000000 (black). Accent colour: #FF3333 (intense red). Secondary colour palette: #E0E0E0, #999999, #505050 (greyscale for depth). Image style and character. Graphic traits: manga-inspired character design is combined with speed lines (beta flash), jagged speech balloons and onomatopoeic sound effects. Shading is handled through classic halftone patterns. Structure and finish: the surface should convey the feel of newspaper or manga paper with visible dot rasters and coarse brushed ink strokes. Composition and light. Layout: a vertical flow of comic panels where figures burst out of the frames and information is presented in dense clusters. Lighting: high ink contrast, dramatic shading via cross-hatching and screentones, plus glowing energy-aura effects. Technical execution. Line work: sharp, emotional lines of varying width, like the trace of a classic steel nib. Shading technique: a combination of halftone dots and linear hatching. Visual effects: motion lines, starbursts and impact flashes. Typography. Headlines: heavy Impact Gothic or a style inspired by graphic sound effects in manga. Body copy: clean, readable sans-serif optimised for placement inside speech balloons. Styling: a white outer glow or outline is used to create contrast against detailed backgrounds. Emphasis: varying text size and uneven edges are used to visualise shouts or particularly important words.

title: Style 19
description: For satirical media-literacy lessons that need a retro comic-book cover feel.
prompt: This design should radiate high energy and satirical sharpness through an action-packed, cinematic retro manner drawing inspiration from classic comic-book worlds. The visual identity rests on a warm bone-white background tone in colour code #F9F7E8, where text in near-total black (#050505) and an intensely yellow accent colour (#FCEE21) create a powerful contrast against a palette of deep red (#E62E2D), saturated blue (#2A5CAA), skin-toned beige (#D4A578) and earthy brown (#5C4535). The image style is characterised by a sequential narrative with panels, speech balloons and jagged explosion fields, where the figures are given exaggerated superhero anatomy and defined by heavy black outlines. The texture should simulate grainy vintage print on newsprint with hints of ink bleed and entirely flat colour fills without gradients. The composition consists of a dense grid of comic panels beneath a dominant main headline, where dynamic action poses are allowed to overlap the panels to create movement. The lighting is flat but dramatic with solid black shadows in inked chiaroscuro style and expressive lines of varying thickness. Onomatopoeic sound effects such as "BUM!" and "PRASK!" in jagged star forms reinforce the visual expression. Typographically, hand-drawn block letters are used for headlines and an all-caps, hand-lettering-inspired comic text in the speech balloons, where the letters have a worn character and a deliberately irregular placement to reinforce the analogue feel.

title: Style 20
description: For middle-school storytelling exercises built around classic action-comic panels.
prompt: This design should be imbued with an energetic, satirical tone full of speed and a distinct retro-cinematic feel. The visual identity rests on a warm bone-white background colour (#F9F7E8) with text in deep black (#050505) and a luminous yellow accent colour (#FCEE21), supported by a palette consisting of red (#E62E2D), blue (#2A5CAA), skin-toned beige (#D4A578) and earthy brown (#5C4535). The imagery is characterised by a sequential storytelling style with comic panels, speech balloons and jagged explosion clouds, where the figures are given exaggerated superhero anatomy and defined by heavy black outlines. The texture should simulate old newsprint with distinct paper structure and a discreet sense of ink bleed, while the colouring is kept entirely flat without any gradients. The composition consists of a dense grid of panels beneath a dominant main headline, where dynamic action poses are allowed to overlap the panels to create movement and life. The lighting is flat but dramatic with heavy, solid black shadows in classic inked chiaroscuro style and expressive outlines of varying line thickness. Onomatopoeic sound effects such as "PANG!" and "BOM!" in jagged star forms reinforce the visual expression. Typographically, hand-drawn block letters are used for headlines and an all-caps, hand-lettering-inspired comic text in the speech balloons, where the letters have a worn character and deliberately irregular placement to reinforce the authentic analogue feel.

title: Style 21
description: For high-contrast classroom posters that need to shout a single clear message.
prompt: This design should radiate a heroic, energetic tonality that combines an authoritative character with an engaging nostalgic feel, deeply rooted in classic comic-book aesthetics. The visual base rests on an intensely yellow background (#FFDD00) where deep black text (#000000) and a powerful red accent colour (#FF3333) create immediate visual impact. The imagery is defined by heavy black ink outlines and shadow patterns in the form of Ben-Day dots, complemented by speech balloons, dramatic action explosions and dynamic motion lines framing the different comic panels. The surface should emulate a vintage daily newspaper with clear halftone rasters and flat colour fills broken up by heavy, graphic shadows. The composition is centred on a heroic main subject forming the focal point, from which informative references radiate outward in the form of dynamic clouds, jagged speech balloons and short comic strips. The lighting is flat and illustrative with high-contrast, hard shadows and a distinct edge light to define volume, while the line work consists of bold ink strokes of varying width emulating hand-drawn comic artwork. The background is reinforced by sunburst effects and halftone gradients creating depth and pulling the gaze toward the centre, where playful sketched icons are also integrated directly into the narrative flow. Typographically, a massive, condensed display style is used for headlines in all caps, often with three-dimensional effects or extra thick outlines, while body copy is kept in a readable sans-serif in classic comic style. To emphasise important points, italic or slanted text is used, placed inside white or colourful speech balloons, ensuring the message appears both authoritative and lively.

title: Style 22
description: For film-analysis and drama lessons inspired by retro sequential storytelling.
prompt: Atmosphere and tonality: the aesthetic should be imbued with a lively and satirical feel that is markedly action-oriented with a distinct retro-cinematic undertone. Graphic Profile. Background shade: #F9F7E8. Primary text colour: #050505. Accent colour: #FCEE21. Supporting colour palette: #E62E2D, #2A5CAA, #D4A578, #5C4535. Imagery and execution. Defining traits: the composition is built on narrative panels in sequence with classic speech balloons and pointy explosion clouds. Use heavy black outlines and exaggerated superhero anatomy to reinforce the graphic expression. Texture and surface: the surface should simulate old-fashioned newsprint with visible grain and ink that appears to have bled into the fibres. The colouring should consist of solid, flat colour fields without any gradients or soft transitions whatsoever. Composition and lighting: use a dense multi-panel grid with a dominant main headline at the top. Create dynamism by letting action poses overlap the panels. Lighting should be flat and illustrative with deep, solid black shadows (ink-based chiaroscuro). Lines and special effects: work with thick, expressive ink lines varying in weight. Include onomatopoeia such as 'BAM!' and 'ZAP' placed in jagged star forms. Typography. Headlines: hand-drawn, heavy block text. Body copy: hand-lettered comic-book typeface in all caps. Style: the letters should have a weathered, bold character with a deliberately irregular placement inside the speech balloons.

title: Style 23
description: For manga-loving tweens when a tough topic needs a chibi narrator to soften it.
prompt: This design should be imbued with an energetic, expressive tone that is deeply narrative and strongly educational with a dramatic undertone. The visual identity is built on a clean white base (#FFFFFF) with deep black text and accents (#000000, #1A1A1A), complemented by a scale of greys from dark grey to near white to create depth. The imagery draws inspiration from dynamic manga series with characters in chibi or shonen style acting out the scenarios through an asymmetrical panel structure with diagonal cuts guiding the eye's movement from a chaotic problem toward a clear solution. To create movement and impact, powerful speed lines, hard-hitting explosions and integrated process diagrams are used, all executed with a traditional ink aesthetic where shading is handled through classic raster dots and varying line thickness like a G-pen. The composition exploits high-contrast lighting in black and white with abstract background patterns alternating between darkness and radiance depending on the mood, while visual metaphors such as lightbulbs, gears and sweat drops in jagged speech balloons further clarify the message. Typographically, heavy brush strokes or distorted styles are used for headlines and sound effects in hand-drawn style, while body copy is kept in a clean, rounded sans-serif inside the speech balloons for optimal readability, where important concepts are emphasised through bold weight, larger size and uneven outlines.

title: Style 24
description: For computer-science units and maker-club posters with a nostalgic arcade vibe.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "retro arcade pixelpunk" infographic that looks like a 16-bit game / CRT screen with a clear pixel aesthetic, scanlines and a faint screen glow: a dark background, neon frames and glitch pixels in the corners and edges, plus a light vignette as if the image were shown on an old monitor. At the top, place a large title in chunky pixel font with a strong colour gradient (cyan→magenta→yellow) and a glowing outline, like an arcade banner; beneath the title, the layout should be split into two large sections side by side: the left half the "problem" part in darker, more chaotic neon (purple/blue) with pixelated lightning bolts, warning icons and small scene panels, and the right half the "solution" part in a lighter, more hopeful pixel world (green/blue) with a pixelated landscape background and three distinct step cards. Build the left side as a vertical stack of 3–4 pixel panels with neon-framed borders, small pixelated characters/robots, UI badges (e.g. "health bars", small dialogue bubbles, warning signs) and short text strips in pixel font; include small "consequence" boxes and icons such as coins, shield, documents and warning triangle, all in the same 16-bit style. In the middle between the halves, place a large pixelated "risk path": a neon zigzag path or energy stream filled with question marks leading from left to right, with glow and small particles, like a game track. On the right, place three equally sized step panels in a row or in a 2x2-like block with clear numerals (1–3) and headings in pixel font, each with a large simple pixel icon (e.g. magnifying glass, brain/shield, search box) and a small "supporting detail" text strip beneath; use brighter colours, green fields, blue sky and clear checkmark symbols to signal "safe". The colour palette should be pure pixelpunk: neon cyan, magenta, purple, electric blue, lime green and warm yellow, with clear 1–2 pixelated shadow levels (no soft photo gradient), and all graphics should have pixel edges and consistent tile/pixel resolution. Add small decorative retro elements: pixelated stars, glitch blocks, "HUD" corner markers, and a discreet frame around the whole image resembling a game-console overlay. Avoid vector cleanliness, photorealism, hand-drawn sketches and modern minimalist UI — the goal is a gamified, arcade-like pixel infographic with CRT feel, neon frames, pixel typography, scene panels on the left, three action steps on the right and a neon question-mark path tying them together.

title: Style 25
description: For side-by-side tool comparisons and structured decision briefs to colleagues.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal tech comparison infographic in modern "UI cards/dashboard" style. The background should be light (white to very light grey) with subtle shadows. Layout: Top: a large, centred heading in bold sans-serif. Below the heading: a row of 3 equally wide column cards side by side. Each card has rounded corners, soft drop shadow and clear internal padding. Each card should have a top header with a colour gradient and a small icon badge. Directly beneath: a short "tagline/vibe" row (1 sentence). Middle of the card: a larger hero illustration/icon in semi-flat tech style (sticker/emoji feel) representing the card's theme generically. Beneath the hero: 2–3 clear blocks with small headings (bold text) and short bullets (max 2–3 lines per block). At the bottom: a row of small process/pipeline boxes (3–5 small boxes) in UI style with short labels, showing a flow from left to right. Style & graphics: "Card UI" aesthetic: rounded corners, thin separators, pill badges, small icons, consistent spacing. Illustrations in flat/semi-flat vector with soft gradients and a light sheen (not photorealism). Colour palette: three distinct colour themes for the cards (e.g. blue, green, purple/pink). Gradient in the header and mild gradient as the background inside each card. Text dark grey/black for high readability. Typography: sans-serif throughout, clear hierarchy, many short lines, easy to scan. Negative instructions: avoid vintage paper, blueprint line art, dark neon HUD, photorealism, hand-drawn sketch, chaotic collage layout, long paragraphs without block structure.

title: Style 26
description: For mid-primary science explainers where the phenomenon sits clearly at the centre.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal modern science explainer infographic with a light, clean background (white to very light grey) and lots of whitespace. Layout: Top: a large main headline in bold sans-serif, centred. Below it, a smaller subtitle in lighter weight. In the middle: a large circular hero illustration (abstract natural phenomenon/object), soft 3D feel with swirling gradients and clear depth. Left side: 3 bullet blocks stacked vertically. Right side: 3 bullet blocks stacked vertically. Each bullet block has a small icon/mini-illustration to the left of the text, a short heading and 1–2 sentences of body copy. Add thin, discreet callout lines from some bullet blocks to relevant parts of the hero illustration. Illustration style: polished digital illustration with soft gradients (semi-3D but not photorealistic). No hard outlines; use subtle shadows and soft highlights for separation. Icons and hero should match in style: same material feel and colour depth. Colour palette: base: white/light grey. Accents: deep blue and violet/purple for the hero, with small touches of warm orange/gold in secondary icons for contrast. Overall premium and calm, not neon. Typography & graphic details: sans-serif, clear hierarchy, high readability. Thin grey connector lines. No heavy framing; minimalist layout. Negative instructions: avoid dark background, neon HUD style, vintage paper, hand-drawn sketch, coarse grunge, excessive gradients, photorealism, chaotic diagrams.

title: Style 27
description: For biology and health lessons that benefit from a clean clinical diagnostic feel.
prompt: Create a modern, clinical medical/tech infographic in a wide horizontal layout (16:9). The background should be white to very light blue-grey, with subtle soft light patches and discreet bokeh/glow. Composition: a large centred hero illustration (round/oval shape) divided vertically into two halves. The left half should feel more "internal/schematic/diagnostic" with thin lines, a diagram feel and subtle technical pattern. The right half should feel more "external/optical" with softer shapes, light reflections and glossy gradients. The hero motif should have a soft halo/glow and a few discreet star/spark highlight points. Typography: at the top, a large main headline in bold sans-serif, centred. Beneath it: two section titles in all caps (left and right), also sans-serif. Body copy should be short and easy to read. Callouts & icons: on the left and right sides there should be 2–3 bullet-like callouts with round icon badges (simple pictograms in circles), a short heading line and 1–2 lines of supporting text. Also include a simple bar graph at the bottom of one of the columns with rounded ends and a gradient fill. Illustration style: vector-based, clean and modern, soft gradients, slight 3D depth via shadows and highlights (not photorealistic). Rounded forms, high polish, "Apple Health/biotech" feel. Colour palette: base in white and light blue-grey. Accents in teal/turquoise, cyan, deep blue, with small yellow/gold highlights. Keep everything soft and clinical, no neon colours. Other: lots of air, clear grid, consistent spacing, professional infographic finish. Negative instructions (add at the bottom): avoid photorealism, hard black outlines, serif typefaces, vintage paper, heavy grunge, extremely strong contrasts, neon/UV aesthetic, messy collage layout, overly detailed illustrations.

title: Style 28
description: For book-club handouts and literature units that want a dignified vintage-textbook finish.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal editorial infographic in "modern vintage textbook" style. Use a parchment/bone-white paper background with subtle grainy print texture. The layout should be strictly organised in blocks/panels with clear hierarchy: a large centred serif main headline at the top, a short subheading beneath the headline, and several sections with serif section headings and short paragraphs of body copy. The imagery should consist of flat vector illustrations with clear outlines, simplified forms and soft shading (light drop shadow) so that objects feel like cut-out elements on paper. Place icon clusters (e.g. generic symbols/objects/emblems) above or next to each text panel. Create a two-tone composition: the left part light/neutral on parchment, the right part with large accent panels in dark navy and brick red/oxblood. Add discreet radial chart lines/rings in the background near the centre for an analytical infographic feel. Use sweeping ribbons/curves as visual connections between sections, and a stylised crack/cleft that graphically divides two areas. Colour palette: bone-white/parchment, warm grey, sand beige; accents in navy and brick red; small accents in ochre/gold and soot grey. Keep the colours muted and printed, not neon. Typography: large classic serif for the main headline, smaller serif for subheadings, readable body copy. Clear margins, even grid, consistent spacing. The final result should feel like a professional textbook infographic: clean, educational, icon-based, with a subtle paper feel and printed finish. Negative instructions (feel free to add): avoid photorealism, 3D render, strong gloss reflections, neon colours, futuristic UI style, chaotic layout, excessive gradients, hand-drawn sketch style, comic cartoon with large faces.

title: Style 29
description: For field trips, outdoor-learning guides, and nature-study packs with a parks-and-rec charm.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal vintage field-guide/national-park infographic with a clear poster feel. Use a warm beige/cream paper background with subtle grainy paper texture and a light "print" finish. Layout: place a thin frame around the entire design. Top: a large centred serif main headline and a thin line beneath. Build the rest in 3 distinct horizontal bands with consistent grid and strong order: Upper band: 5–6 small panel cards in a row (light sage/olive tone) with large dark-grey silhouettes (flat icons) and a short heading + a small "key fact" badge in each card. Middle band: one or two diagram blocks in vintage style (long horizontal bars/lines, simple gauge/indicator with a needle), with small silhouette icons at the ends. Bottom band: a "snapshot" row with repeated small silhouettes as pictograms (small icons in a series) and a row of numbers beneath — keep it as a general infographic layout (not subject-specific). Illustration style: use flat silhouette graphics (1–2 colours), minimal detail, no photorealism. Forms should be sturdy and recognisable, with soft corners and clean edges. No 3D effects; at most a subtle shadow for separation. Typography: classic serif for headings and section titles (ideally with a small-caps feel). Body copy small, tight and fact-box-like. Section headings centred with small decorative flourishes/lines on the sides. Colour palette: warm paper (beige/cream), muted sage/olive in panel backgrounds, dark grey/charcoal for silhouettes and text, small accents in sepia/brown. Finish: everything should feel like a printed visitor-centre poster: clean, educational, retro, calm, with subtle paper texture and clear frames. Negative instructions: avoid modern neon/tech style, strong gradients, photorealism, 3D render, glossy highlights, sans-serif "app" UI, messy collage layout, strongly saturated colours.

title: Style 30
description: For growth-mindset, habit-change, or transition lessons told as a before-and-after character story.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "before → after" instructional graphic in clean anime/manga-inspired illustration style with soft, warm beige backgrounds and a simple layout. On the left, place a large full-body or half-body portrait illustration of a young anime character in clean line art: thin dark outlines, soft cel-shading (1–2 shadow levels), warm colours, and minimal background detail; the character should have large expressive eyes, simplified facial features and slightly tousled hair, in a relaxed pose. Beneath the left image, a simple label in black sans-serif should sit centred (e.g. "Input"), and between left and right a clear black arrow with a soft curve should point from left to right. On the right, place an "output" panel resembling a grid of 2 rows × 3 columns with six equally sized rectangular frames (thin grey lines) on the same warm beige background, where the same anime character is shown in six different full-body poses in each frame; each frame should have a short pose title beneath in small black sans-serif, and the heading/labels should be consistent and clean. Beneath the entire right-hand panel, a larger label in black sans-serif should sit centred (e.g. "Output"). The whole image should have lots of whitespace, a neutral colour scale, no gradients in the background, no photorealistic elements and no UI cards with shadows; it should feel like a simple instructional page from a textbook/exercise sheet with anime illustrations, consistent line art, soft cel-shading and clear left-to-right transformation via arrow and labels.

title: Style 31
description: For edtech tutorials and digital-skills lessons where multimedia steps need a neon flow.
prompt: Create a modern infographic with "tech tutorial" imagery: a dark, soft gradient background in blue-green/teal fading toward near-black at the edges (subtle vignette), high contrast and a clear neon-glow aesthetic; divide the composition into two distinct zones: the left side shows a stylised "UI/whiteboard" panel with rounded corners and a glassy/semi-transparent look (glassmorphism) where three simple, flat neon icons are stacked vertically (video-play icon, image/landscape icon, sound/waves icon), each icon in its own colour tone (cyan/green/purple) with soft outer glow and thin lines; from the icons, three light rays/energy flows (gradient trails) should sweep diagonally in toward the centre, like colourful fibre-optic ribbons with small particles/sparkles and motion blur, pointing toward a central "hub"; in the centre: a clean, rounded square app icon (white plate with soft shadow) with a simple, colourful four-point/diamond shape in the middle, surrounded by a thin network pattern of nodes and lines (constellation feel) in cyan/white with a faint glow; the right side: a large, friendly portrait photograph of a smiling person in medium shot close to the camera, well-lit with soft key light and slight rim light, natural skin tone, sharp focus on the face and a slightly softer background, dressed in a simple sweater, placed so the face dominates the right third; add a discreet edge separation (outline/glow) around the person so they pop from the background; overall colour and light treatment: crisp sharpness, clear edges, subtle bloom on neon elements, no small text or details requiring reading, clean and educational iconography, dynamic arrows/streams leading the eye from left icons → centre hub → face; no logos, no watermarks, no UI controls, no timestamps.

title: Style 32
description: For teacher-coach videos, AI-verktyg intros, and polished tool showcases on big screens.
prompt: Create a modern, minimalist infographic in 16:9 with "tech coach" imagery: a clean studio feel, high contrast, lots of negative space and super-clear visual hierarchy. Composition: three main blocks — (1) giant headline text in the upper left corner in white, bold sans-serif (all caps, lots of air between letters, slight shadow/contrast edge for readability), (2) a large, square app/product icon object on the left side placed on a simple, warm wooden surface/pedestal, the icon in semi-3D with soft glass/metal material, rounded corners, subtle mirror sheen and a colour gradient moving from cyan/blue to purple/pink with soft glow, (3) a friendly, credible portrait on the right (chest-up shot) of a person with glasses and a smile, evenly lit, sharp focus, soft background blur. Background: simple dark blue/teal gradient with a light vignette and soft falloff behind the subject, no distracting patterns. Connecting element: a clear, hand-drawn-feeling but "clean" cyan arrow pointing from the portrait to the icon (thick line, rounded tip, slight glow), to create a simple "this is what it's about" relationship. Style rules: photorealistic portrait + stylised 3D icon, no complex collage, no small text, no diagrams; max 1–2 words in the headline. Colour palette: white text + cyan accent + dark blue background + warm wood tone; lighting soft and professional, high sharpness on face and icon, thumbnail-optimised readability at small size.

title: Style 33
description: For debate-lesson openers that split a question into hopeful and cautionary halves.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "editorial split-screen infographic" in modern newsroom/policy-report style with a clear two-way split: the left half light and hopeful (cool blue/teal palette) and the right half dark and warning-toned (dark navy/charcoal with orange/red accents). At the top, place a large centred main headline in bold sans-serif on a white/light background with a smaller context subline beneath in a thinner sans-serif; beneath the headline, the entire surface should be divided by a vertical centre line or hard transition so the left side feels like "promise" and the right like "peril", each with its own clear section title in all caps in the respective accent colour (teal on the left, orange on the right). Inside each half, 3 clear content blocks stack vertically with headings, 1–2 lines of microcopy and a large iconic illustration per block in semi-flat vector with soft gradients, light shadows and clear outlines; the left-hand illustrations should use symbols as data/insight metaphors (magnifier, glowing "mountain of data", control room, global network) and the right-hand illustrations should use warning metaphors (robot with a warning symbol, "incorrect"-stamped news cards, fabricated documents, risk icons, warning triangles), but keep everything generic and not subject-specific in the text content. Use a diagonal or zigzag shape running through the middle (like a graph/rollercoaster) to visually tie the halves together, with an upward trend on the left and a downward/unstable shape on the right, in a soft gradient transitioning from teal to orange. Typography should be highly scannable: headlines in bold sans-serif, body copy in light grey/dark grey depending on background, important words can be marked with accent colour, and use thin dividers, small icon badges and clear margins for a report feel. The backgrounds should reinforce the tone: the left side light with subtle geometric forms, the right side dark with faint "newsprint/paper stack" silhouettes at low opacity, plus small confetti/warning particles in orange. Keep the style consistent: semi-flat vector, light 3D through gradients, no photos, no hand-drawn lines, no neon HUD glow; the goal is a professional, publishable policy infographic with clear promise-vs-peril contrast, three blocks per side, a central zigzag trend form and clean editorial typographic hierarchy.

title: Style 34
description: For design-thinking handouts and editorial reflection prompts for upper-secondary pupils.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "minimalist premium tech visual" in an airy editorial style with lots of negative space, a white to very light-grey background and subtle paper/grain texture plus a soft vignette giving depth. Use typography that combines a large elegant serif display headline on the far left (thin, high-contrast, lots of kerning) with a short subtitle in small sans-serif beneath; keep everything sober, like a design studio presentation slide. On the right half, three small semi-transparent isometric cubes should sit at the top (glass/ice feel) with very discreet line icons on their sides and low opacity, floating with soft shadows. Beneath the cubes, an "intelligence layer" should be visualised as an organic, flowing network form of interwoven curves in a cyan→teal→purple gradient, with rounded nodes (small circles) and a light glow/bloom, plus small particles/dust around the lines; the lines should feel like soft light streams rather than hard vector arrows. Beneath this, an "architecture" level should appear as a technical blueprint-like diagram surface in isometric perspective: thin grey construction lines, dimension lines and coordinate markers, with a clean flow diagram of loops and arrows colour-coded in blue and purple, with rounded rectangular nodes, small labels in micro-text and arrows showing circular processes; everything should be extremely clean, fine-lined and slightly transparent so it feels integrated into the background. Place small discreet side labels along the right edge in all caps relevant to the content (e.g. if the content is about AI, it could read "AI MODELS", "INTELLIGENCE LAYER", "ARCHITECTURE") in thin sans-serif and light-grey colour, plus a small minimalist star/sparkle icon in one corner for balance. The colour palette should be very controlled: neutral greys for grid and text, and only the cyan/teal/purple accent gradient for flows and nodes; no strong colour blocks, no heavy shadows, no neon HUD overload and no hand-drawn style. The overall impression should be a high-end, Apple/DeepMind-like "design systems" visual: elegant serif headline, floating glass cubes, organic light-network form, and a fine-meshed blueprint diagram in isometric perspective, with maximum air and sophisticated minimalism.

title: Style 35
description: For Nordic-clean comparison briefs used in parent evenings or staff meetings.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "minimalist museum/print infographic" on a light-grey/off-white background with lots of negative space, a thin grid and an extremely clean typographic hierarchy. The style should be Scandinavian/editorial: small, well-placed text blocks in dark-grey sans-serif, discreet headings in a larger serif or clean sans (left-aligned main headline in the upper left), and very thin lines for axes, separator strokes and callouts. The visualisation should be dominated by a row of small, realistic but softly illustrated animal/object figures in profile (natural-history illustration with a watercolour/gouache feel but toned down), placed along a horizontal scale across the middle of the image, where each figure is accompanied by a circular data point (large flat circles) in warm orange with a percentage/number in white or dark text; use multiple circle sizes to show magnitude and keep the circles perfectly round and graphically clean. On the right, place a large, semi-transparent anatomical/biological illustration in profile (e.g. a head/organ) as a "hero" in low contrast, with thin annotation lines and small labels in grey, so it feels integrated but does not take over. At the bottom, place a row of small icon pictograms (monochrome line icons) and short labels as a "legend strip", evenly distributed, with thin vertical separators and lots of air. The colour palette should be strict: light-grey background, dark-grey text, warm orange as the only strong accent, plus possibly a very discreet beige/light-brown tone in the illustrations; no strong gradients, no shadows like UI cards and no neon colours. The layout should feel like a high-class magazine infographic: precise alignment, small figure numbers, fine line weights, consistent columns and calm rhythm; avoid hand-drawn sketchnote, glassmorphism, dark background and excessive colour — the goal is a clean, elegant, data-driven print infographic with small illustrations, orange data-point circles, a large faded anatomical inset on the right and an icon row at the bottom.

title: Style 36
description: For hub-and-spoke handouts on trends, roles, or values — magazine-clean.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal modern "lifestyle trend infographic" in a clean magazine/SaaS style with a white background, generous whitespace, and a central hub-and-spoke composition. At the top, place a large centered title in bold sans-serif (black/dark grey) with a short year/theme in uppercase, and below it a large circular hero portrait illustration in the middle (semi-flat vector with soft gradients and a subtle 3D feel, clean contours, stylized fashion illustration). Around the center, arrange 6–8 rounded text panels / organic "blob" shapes like petals, each with its own soft pastel gradient (mint green, lavender, light blue, coral, yellow, teal) and a short headline in uppercase/bold sans-serif plus 1–2 lines of description; the panels should connect visually to the center through their shape (overlapping/touching the central circle) rather than with lines, so the composition reads as a cohesive cluster. Each panel should have a large themed illustration nearby (e.g. product objects, an accessory, an iconic detail) in the same semi-flat vector style with soft shadows, subtle highlights, and a clean silhouette; keep the objects colorful but controlled, with certain parts glossy (plastic/glass) via subtle gradients. Also include a few small UI-like icons (e.g. an app screen, a recycling symbol, a tech symbol) as stickers/illustrations, but without tipping into technical HUD; everything should feel like a modern trend report. The color palette should be bright and optimistic: white base, pastel blobs, and accent colors in turquoise, purple, coral, and yellow; text should be dark for readability, and use mild drop shadow around objects and blobs for separation. Typography should be consistent sans-serif with clear hierarchy (title largest, panel headings medium, body copy small), and the layout should be scannable and social-media-friendly; avoid vintage paper, hand-drawn sketchnote, neon glow, and photorealism — the goal is a clean, modern, semi-flat trend infographic with a central portrait hub and color-coded theme bubbles around it.

title: Style 37
description: For climate and geology spreads pupils will actually want to read twice.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "National Geographic-style climate science spread" with a clean white background and a blend of photorealistic 3D geology, technical cutaways, and editorial typography. The layout should feel like a printed magazine spread: a large left-aligned headline in elegant serif, a short standfirst in smaller text, and several small text columns with subheads in uppercase/sans-serif; use generous margins, a strict grid, and plenty of whitespace. The main motif should be a large photorealistic 3D-rendered landmass / terrain slice in perspective (like a cut-out model) with a semi-transparent "ice/layer" cap on top, where one large corner is folded/lifted like a paper flap to reveal the layers underneath (cutaway/peel-back effect); show clear material contrast between ice (light cyan, translucent) and bedrock (dark stone, textured), with soft shadows and realistic lighting. On the left, place a tall rectangular vertical cross-section illustration (a technical 3D cube/column) that shows layers descending in a block model with thin label lines and small labels, like a geological core sample; use light blue tones for ice/water and neutral greys for rock, with very fine contour lines. Spread thin annotation lines (grey) that point from text to specific points on the 3D model and the cross-section column, with small dot anchors and discreet arrows; keep labels small and precise. Along the bottom, place a band-like section with several small mini-graphics and micro-diagrams in a row (e.g. small map insets, a simple area chart/legend, small timeline steps), all with thin frames, small figure numbers, and short captions, like a "figure strip" on a magazine page. The color palette should be muted and scientific: white background, black/dark grey text, light cyan/ice blue for ice elements, neutral grey/brown for rock, and very sparing accent colors for markings; avoid neon, glassmorphism, hand-drawn sketchnote, and flat cartoon icons — the goal is a sober, photorealistic, editorial science infographic with a 3D cutaway terrain, a vertical cross-section column, thin annotation lines, and a bottom edge with small figures and captions in a strict magazine layout.

title: Style 38
description: For edtech and AI-in-school introductions aimed at mixed staff groups.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal modern "edtech explainer infographic" in a clean, light, and professional style with lots of whitespace and a soft, friendly premium feel. The background should be white with subtle pastel gradients and large soft color shapes (transparent circles/halos) that create separation between the left and right sections without hard frames. At the top, a large centered headline in bold sans-serif should have a two-color gradient in the text (warm red/orange transitioning into teal/blue) and a smaller subtitle in dark grey/black; typography should be modern with a clear hierarchy. The layout should be two main columns: the left side presents the "problem" with a large central illustration (semi-flat vector with soft gradients) of a person and stacked books, overlaid by a large translucent pie/Venn-like overlay with two percentage labels in large type, and a speech bubble above containing a short question; below that, a short bullet/statement with a small accent dot. The right side presents a "3-step workflow" section with a clear heading and three steps stacked vertically, each with a small icon (book, magnifying glass, document, robot, etc.) in a consistent rounded app-icon style with mild glow and gradient, followed by a short bold step heading and 1–2 lines of explanatory text; between the steps, a visual flow with soft arrows or a thin curved gradient line running through the middle like a "path". In the middle between the columns, place a larger "tech" illustration (e.g. a brain inside a screen or a dark purple panel) acting as a visual hub, with subtle neon-like glow but still in a light, clean style (not dark HUD). In the lower right, place a small icon row/cluster with 3 round badges (e.g. audio/visual/quiz) and a stylized profile-head icon with a brain in gradient colors, all in the same semi-flat, softly shadowed vector style. The color palette should be controlled and modern: teal/cyan, purple, blue, and warm coral/orange as accents, with soft gradients and light shadows; avoid hard black outlines, vintage paper, hand-drawn sketches, and neon/HUD overkill. The overall impression should be a social-media-friendly, print-ready, well-structured infographic with large headlines, clear step blocks, consistent iconography, soft gradient transitions, and strong readability.

title: Style 39
description: For bold science-feature handouts with a strong black-banner magazine entrance.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "National Geographic-style editorial science spread" with a clean white background, a strict magazine grid, and a bold black header banner running across the entire top. In the header, place a large dramatic headline in high-contrast serif display in white uppercase (very large, tight tracking, newspaper feel), with a small kicker/section tag in smaller sans-serif above or beside it; also add a small text column in the upper right corner on a black background with a short headline and a few lines of body copy. Below the header, the main canvas should be minimalist and light with lots of whitespace and thin grey guide lines; let the middle section be dominated by a large, ultra-realistic but illustrated rendering of an appropriate object/animal/human tied to the content of the sources, placed over a soft "ground" shadow, with fine texture and soft studio lighting (no hard outline). Place 2–3 smaller figures in the same style along the lower edge for scale comparison, plus a discreet human silhouette in light grey as a scale reference on the left; the figures should stand on the same baseline as an exhibition row. Place three short text blocks in columns above or around the main figure (left, center, right) with headings in uppercase and body copy in small serif/sans, all in dark grey for a sober editorial feel. Along the bottom, place a horizontal "figure strip" with 3–4 small anatomical inset illustrations (e.g. skulls/sections) in fine line drawing with transparent color overlays in muted blue/red/yellow tones, with short captions underneath and thin separator lines; keep them as scientific figures rather than icons. Include small page notes such as a page number/section title at the bottom for print feel. The color palette should be extremely controlled: black header, white background, grey text/lines, and very sparing accent colors only in the small anatomical insets; avoid neon, glassmorphism, hand-drawn sketchnote, and colorful diagrams — the goal is an elegant, museum-like printed magazine layout with a black headline band, large realistic animal illustrations on a white canvas, discreet column text, and a bottom strip of small anatomical figures.

title: Style 40
description: For reflection journals and philosophy lessons with a warm lo-fi printed feel.
prompt: Atmosphere and feel: The aesthetic is instructional and tactile with an inviting, intellectual tone. It conveys complex logic through a nostalgic lo-fi aesthetic reminiscent of older printed matter. Visual Profile Background color: #FDFBF6 (warm white paper tone) Primary text color: #1C1C5E (deep navy) Accent color: #E63E85 (vivid cerise) Secondary palette: #E8AF3F, #5C66B2, #242D45, #2E2C87. Imagery and Style Technical characteristics: Illustrations that simulate offset printing with varying transparency across layers (multiply effect). Information is framed by speech bubbles and reinforced by metaphorical icons (e.g. brains, gears, light bulbs). The design has a characteristic graphic misregistration where contours are not perfectly in phase with the color fields. Texture and surface: A distinct paper texture combined with ink bleed, subtle raster patterns, and lines with a rustic, stamp-like finish. The linework is organic with natural variations in thickness, like the tracks of a felt-tip pen. Composition and light: The structure follows a logical flowchart with a top-down hierarchy. Information is gathered in clusters connected by directional arrows, with colored background blocks defining the different zones. Lighting is entirely flat (typical of 2D print); depth is suggested only through overlapping colors and simple shading effects. Typography Main headlines: A heavy geometric sans-serif (in the style of Japanese Gothic). Body text: A rounded sans-serif in medium weight, optimized for good readability even at small sizes. Rendering: The text has a subtle blur or ink bleed to blend with the analog paper feel.

title: Style 41
description: For concept-map handouts and revision summaries that benefit from calm technical structure.
prompt: This design should convey a pedagogical, authoritative, and precisely structured feel with a clear schematic focus. The visual identity rests on a near-white background in the tone #FBFBFB with text in a dark grey shade (#222222) and a muted blue accent color (#7FA1C3), complemented by a secondary palette of #E6B89C, #9CCEA6, #CCCCCC, and #555555. The illustration style is distinguished by isometric 3D blocks and hierarchical node trees with fills that have a stippled texture, connected by dashed flow lines. The surface should mimic matte textbook paper with subtle noise or grain on the filled shapes. The composition is divided into two columns: a detailed text description on the left and the illustrative diagrams on the right, separated by horizontal dividers. The lighting is flat and diagrammatic with only soft shading on the 3D elements to create depth without losing technical clarity. Linework is uniformly thin, clean, and consistent, while arrows are rendered as dashed curves with small triangular tips. Typographically, a bold sans-serif in Gothic style is used for headlines and a legible sans-serif in medium weight for body text, where horizontal lines separate semantic sections and key terms are marked in bold to reinforce the hierarchy.

title: Style 42
description: For learning-journey handouts where a winding path guides pupils through stages.
prompt: Overall design settings Tonality: The mood should be professional and optimistic with an airy feel focused on being informative. Graphic identity Background color: #F4F9FB Text color: #667882 Accent color: #5DB6E3 Secondary colors: #3B6075, #91AAB8, #FFFFFF. Imagery and style Aesthetic characteristics: Flat vector iconography combined with a central, curving path that functions as a timeline. Use simplified geometric shapes and dashed guide lines. Texture and surface: The surface should be smooth and matte with a digital vector feel reminiscent of paper-like simplicity. Composition and flow: The layout is adapted for vertical scrolling with a central axis running like a spine through the image. Content alternates between left- and right-aligned along the axis. Plenty of whitespace creates balance. The eye is guided downward through a central axis shaped as an S-curve. Lighting and icons: Uniform flat lighting with no shadows at all and high-key brightness. Icons in two tones: sky blue and dark slate grey. Typography Main headlines: A narrow, tall sans-serif (in the style of Bebas Neue or Oswald). Body text: A clean geometric sans-serif (e.g. Open Sans or Roboto). Dates: Formatted in bold in the accent color.

title: Style 43
