In each panel one or two central character silhouettes should stand full-figure in a dramatic pose, with carefully rendered costume/props, and the background should support the era's mood (forest, hall, laboratory/library, futuristic skyline). At the top of each panel a clear era headline in serif (white/warm-white) should sit with a small date line in parentheses beneath; at the bottom of each panel there should be a short caption in smaller serif with a bold title line and 1–2 lines of description, placed on a dark gradient base or overlaid text plate for readability. Use a consistent layout: four equally wide vertical fields, thin separating lines between the panels, clear typographic hierarchy, and a common color-grading look (cinematic, light vignetting, soft bloom in highlights). Avoid flat vector, sketchnote, and minimalist UI cards; the goal is a dramatic, highly detailed, painted timeline poster where the imagery carries the narrative, with clear era headings and short captions, like a premium illustrated history/legend guide.

title: Style 86
description: For fantasy-literature and D&D-style reading quests on a hand-painted adventure map.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "illustrated fantasy map infographic" that looks like a hand-painted storybook map on aged parchment: warm beige paper with light blotches, darker worn edges, subtle grain, and a thin framing that feels like an old map plate. The imagery should be playful and detailed like a children's book/role-playing map: digitally painted gouache/watercolor feel with thin dark contours, soft shadows, muted but rich colors (sage green, sky blue, terracotta, ochre, lavender), and lots of small narrative "life" in the scene. The composition should function as a journey through a landscape with many named places: winding paths, bridges, cliffs, valleys, small villages, towers, gates, forests, rivers, and a coast/harbor, where each place has a small banner label or sign (as on a map) and small speech bubbles/signs with short "tips" in boxes that look like hand-drawn signage. Include lots of small miniature characters and creatures (travelers, craftsmen, guardians, small animals/owls/figures) performing activities along the route, plus decorative elements like floating symbols, magical spirals, sparkles, and small icons that reinforce the "fantasy explainer" feel without becoming neon. Typography should be classic and map-like: an elegant serif title at the top (centered) and smaller handwritten/serif labels on banners for the places, with consistent layout and good readability. Also add vertical side columns along the left and right edges containing stacked small "rule/tip" boxes with small icon illustrations beside them (in a margin-note style), all integrated into the map's parchment frame. The overall impression should be a highly detailed, humorous, pedagogical storybook map/atlas where information bits are built in as signs and banners within the environment, with clear flow from a starting point to a destination harbor, without modern UI card aesthetic, without photorealism, and without hard vector lines — just a warm hand-painted map feel, rich storytelling, many small details, and clear labels.

title: Style 87
description: For learning-journey posters that guide pupils step-by-step through a school term.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "cozy edtech journey map" in soft, playful 2.5D illustration style (Pixar-light but flatter) with a bright, optimistic color palette and a storybook nature environment: a green meadow with rounded bushes, trees, and soft hills in the background, subtle atmospheric haze, and small flowers in the foreground; use soft gradients, rounded shapes, and warm daylight lighting without hard shadows. The composition should be a winding "pathway infographic" where a sparkling, neon-like but soft light path (turquoise/white) snakes through the landscape as a clear S-curve from left to right, with small light dots/sparkles along the way and discreet arrows showing direction. Along the path, 5–6 large round "glow bubbles" (circles with a soft cyan halo and glassy edge) should serve as stations; in each bubble there should be a large, clear icon in modern flat/2.5D style (book, brush/art, code screen, clock/checklist, presentation/people), and right next to each bubble a short numbered headline and 1–2 lines of explanatory text in clean sans-serif, with good contrast and a light outline/shadow for readability against the green background. On the left side a larger information box should sit (white or very light panel with rounded corners and thin border) containing a longer text passage; the box should look like a noticeboard/sign with a small "hanging" feel and have a small icon in the corner. Also add small flying micro-icons along the path (camera, chat bubbles, lightbulbs, small papers/planes) as "confetti" to reinforce the theme, but keep them few and well-balanced. At the top centered, a large title in strong sans-serif with a subtle gradient (e.g. turquoise → blue) and a smaller sub-line beneath; the whole design should feel like a friendly workshop/course poster with a clear journey and stops along a magical path. Avoid photorealism, dark backgrounds, hand-drawn sketchnote, and hard neon HUD; the goal is a bright, playful, nature-based journey map with a sparkling winding path, cyan-glow station bubbles, large icons, numbered step texts, and a left-aligned intro panel in modern edtech poster style.

title: Style 88
description: For career-education and future-self units told as a floating pastel fantasy map.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "kawaii fantasy career map" infographic in pastel, dreamy storybook style with a soft airbrush/gouache feel, sparkling star particles, and a magical atmosphere. The background should be a soft sky in gradient (light blue → purple → pink) with fluffy clouds, small sparkling stars, and light bokeh, and across the scene several small floating islands/platforms should hover at various heights, connected by thin light paths, rainbow bridges, or small trails that lead the eye on a journey from left to right. Each island should contain a small vignette with chibi animal characters (cute foxes/cats/rabbits with large eyes and small bodies) in happy poses, plus props symbolizing "job/role" stations (small buildings, podium, microphone, laptop, camera, robots, heart and like icons, crystals, portals); everything should be in the same kawaii illustration style with soft contours, rounded shapes, and subtle cel-shading. At the top center, a large title in bubbly, playful display typography should sit with a mild glow and star ornaments, and each station/island should have a small banner label (rolled parchment-like ribbon) with short headline text in clear sans/serif and a smaller description line beneath, placed so it feels integrated into the scene. Add many small decorative elements: floating icons in small boxes (AI, brain, document), stars, hearts, chat bubbles, small comets/rockets, and sparkling crystals in corners; use soft light rays and glow around important objects for a "magical UI" feel without neon. The color palette should be consistently pastel: lavender, baby blue, mint, peach pink, soft yellow, with white highlights; avoid hard black outlines, dark shadows, and photorealism. The overall impression should be a charming, highly detailed, dreamy infographic that blends narrative map (floating islands + journey) with clear text banners and icon vignettes, like a kawaii storybook/Disney × Sanrio-inspired career-journey poster.

title: Style 89
description: For gamified onboarding and study-skills kickoffs with isometric game-board charm.
prompt: This design should convey a playful and approachable manner that feels both friendly and well-structured by using a gamified aesthetic to simplify complex processes. The visual profile rests on a light ice-blue background (#E8F0FE) with text in a warm dark brown shade (#5D4037) and a mint-green accent color (#69F0AE), complemented by a harmonious pastel palette in shades of pale orange, yellow, turquoise, pink, and soft purple. The imagery builds on an isometric projection with voxel-style characters reminiscent of Minecraft figures, placed on floating island platforms connected by three-dimensional arrows and floating icons like stars and question marks. Surfaces have a smooth digital finish with soft color gradients and clearly defined edge lights that yield clean lines entirely without noise, supported by soft, bright lighting that creates gentle pastel-colored shadows and a dreamy atmosphere. The layout follows a diagonal downward motion from the upper left corner to the lower right to illustrate a linear journey where the various process steps are grouped on isolated platforms. The avatars are designed with a charming low-poly aesthetic and square heads, while sparkles and stars are used to visualize insights or successful solutions. Information carriers such as code screens and speech bubbles are rendered as floating holograms or panels to reinforce the digital game feel. For headlines, a heavy, rounded sans-serif is used with white fill and strong outlines so the text pops against the light backgrounds, while body text is kept in a clean, rounded sans-serif in medium weight for maximum readability.

title: Style 90
description: For geography or earth-science handouts built around a globe and connected bubble panels.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal pedagogical nature infographic in textbook style (friendly, clear, illustrated). The background should be clean white with plenty of air and high readability. Composition: Large title at top left in a clear headline style (classic textbook feel). Top right: a large hero illustration in the form of a globe/overview image with a soft halo and atmospheric color (not photorealistic). Below/emerging from the globe: a series of organic, connected bubble/cloud-shaped panels that serve as levels/sections. Each bubble contains a small scene (landscape + some animals/objects) in the same style. Add a large, sweeping energy-flow ribbon from left to right: yellow → orange gradient, soft edges, clear direction, and motion. Illustration style: Digitally painted, soft brushstrokes, "storybook" but factual. Semi-realistic natural environments (trees, mountains, water, desert, etc.) with simplified shapes and clear silhouettes. Soft shadows, subtle light, mild atmospheric haze. Graphic elements and text: Short callouts and labels placed airily, 1–2 sentences per callout. Small headlines near each bubble section. Arrows/ribbons should be wide, soft, gradient-filled (not hard vector arrows). Color palette: Natural, warm tones: greens, turquoise/blue, sand/ochre, terracotta. Accent in sunshine yellow–orange gradient for flow ribbons and highlights. Keep contrast soft but the text dark and easy to read. Finish: Clear schoolbook/educational infographic: friendly, clean, informative, no grunge, no dark blocks. Negative instructions: Avoid photorealism, hard vector/flat-only without brush feel, neon colors, dark panels, high-tech UI style, chaotic collage, aggressive contrasts, comic-book outlines with thick black lines.

title: Style 91
description: For primary-maths classroom posters with kawaii storybook warmth.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal child-friendly math infographic in "kawaii storybook classroom poster" style with a clear thematic framework and warm, playful illustration: use a soft, hand-illustrated digital gouache/watercolor feel with thin dark contours, subtle paper grain, and a light, sunny color palette (sand beige, sky blue, warm ochre, turquoise, and coral) with soft shadows and rounded shapes. The composition should have a large banner title at the top in a printed, friendly serif/sans-serif in bold style and decorated corners (e.g. small figures or ornaments), plus a decorative outer frame around the whole image filled with small symbols/ornaments in the same theme (like an illustrated "border"). The background should be a simple, idyllic environment in pastel (desert/landscape feel with soft hills, a few iconic silhouettes in the distance, a sun and moon with faces) but keep it clean so the main objects stand out. In the middle there should be 2–3 large main illustrations (e.g. a treasure chest, baskets/containers, a hero figure) and a series of curved, glowing "magical" arrows/energy streams that lead the eye downward to a row of 5 equally sized "result" vignettes at the bottom (five baskets/containers) where each vignette has a clear number in a round badge above; use the same layout logic but without locking to specific numerals. The figures should be cute and pedagogical: chibi proportions, large eyes, friendly smiles, clear thematic clothing/attributes; include 1–2 extra "comic relief" characters (e.g. a small mascot) giving a thumbs up and a speech bubble with a short encouraging phrase, with a thick white bubble and soft shadow. Typography should be large and easy for children to read: headlines in bold, rounded display style, supporting text in simple sans-serif, and a summarizing sentence at the bottom in a clear line with high contrast against the background. Add small sparkles/stars around the magical arrows, use color-coded arrows and discreet glow effects (not neon) to show flow, and keep the whole thing like a print-ready school poster: clean, positive, clear hierarchy, no photorealistic textures, no dark HUD elements, and no minimalist corporate cards — just warm, playful storybook infographic with thematic frame, cute characters, arrows showing distribution, and clear numbers in badges.

title: Style 92
description: For romance-era literature and art-history handouts with Mucha-inspired elegance.
prompt: This design vision should convey an elegant and dreamlike atmosphere with clear influences from classic European romance, giving a soft, whimsical yet pedagogical impression. The visual identity rests on a background in a creamy, ivory white shade (#FDF6F0) where the dark brown main text (#6B4C40) creates warmth, while accents in ochre-gold (#EBC97A) and secondary tones in rose-red (#E88CA0), sky blue (#BFE3F2), and coral (#D96C75) gild the whole. The imagery draws inspiration from Victorian fantasy and ancient mythology, populated by classical cherubs, ornate roses, seashells, and pearls alternating with allegorical scenes. The narrative is tied together by flowing golden ribbons that serve as signposts through an S-shaped narrative path linking three distinct stages, all within an asymmetrically balanced composition framed by decorative floral corners. The surface has the character of matte watercolor paper with soft color transitions and an ethereal sheen, where magical light reflections radiate from central objects such as mirrors or books in otherwise soft, diffuse ambient light. Instead of hard black contours, fine, warm lines in brown and rose tones are used to preserve the delicate feel. Typographically, a sophisticated Mincho-style serif for headlines in gold or pink is combined with a clean handwritten style or rounded sans-serif for body text, reinforcing the elegant and fairytale character.

title: Style 93
description: For vintage schoolroom posters on grammar, nature, or classic facts.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal pedagogical "storybook classroom poster" infographic in warm, hand-illustrated textbook style with a soft gouache/watercolor feel, thin dark contours, subtle paper texture, and a muted vintage palette (teal/sage, warm beige, brown, ochre). The layout should feel like a classic school chart with a decorative ornamental frame around the whole image (curly corners, small thematic symbols at the corners) and a large elegant serif headline at the top with a smaller sub-line beneath, as on an old-fashioned educational poster. Centrally, a large black/gray chalkboard should dominate, with chalk-written formulas/text in white chalk and a few colored chalk accents (light blue, light green) plus faint chalk-dust smears; in front of the board stands an anthropomorphic "teacher" character in friendly storybook style (e.g. an animal in a tweed jacket and glasses) pointing with a stick, with warm expression and clear, soft shadows. On both sides of the board there should be bookshelves/classroom props (books, small details) in the same illustrated style to frame the scene. The bottom should be divided into four large, equally wide step panels (1–4) with rounded corners and clear header ribbons in each panel; each panel contains a small vignette of the teacher character in different poses, a speech bubble or "tip" box, plus simplified diagrams/arrows and color-coded markings that look like hand-drawn notes (thin lines in red/blue/green), but keep the content generic and not subject-specific. Use typography that mixes an elegant serif for the main headline and a clear sans/serif for panel headlines and short explanatory lines; all text should look printed but harmonize with the hand-drawn feel. Add small playful details like an eraser, chalks on the chalk ledge, a small paw/stamp effect on the board, and some papers/notes at the bottom for storytelling. The overall impression should be a curated, illustrated educational poster: warm, charming, museum/textbook feel, clear step-by-step structure, soft colors, subtle textures; avoid photorealism, neon/HUD, flat modern UI cards, and hard vector lines without brush feel.

title: Style 94
description: For craft, sloyd, or design-tech units with an embroidered denim-workshop finish.
prompt: This design profile radiates a craftsmanlike and professional feel that is both tactile and durable, with a distinctly instructional bent. The visual base consists of a deep blue background with color code #223C63, where white text (#FFFFFF) and a golden accent color (#DCB35C) create strong contrast alongside secondary tones in red (#A83232), medium blue (#4D6D91), leather brown (#8E5C3E), and gray (#B0B0B0). The image style is defined by a true-to-life denim backdrop adorned with embroidered text effects, diagrams designed as sewn-on fabric patches, copper rivets, and raw, frayed edges. Textures shift between rough cotton weave, metallic hardness, raised thread relief, and soft felt details. The composition is built as a vertical stack divided into modular compartments to govern the information flow, with sections separated by stitched, dashed lines. Lighting consists of soft ambient light from above that creates discreet drop shadows under the fabric patches and gives the typography an embossed effect. The framing consists of a frayed denim edge with copper rivets at the corners, while embroidered yellow arrows and dashed stitching serve as visual connectors. Information is presented on lighter denim patches and red fabric labels. Typographically, a bold sans-serif with embroidered relief is used for headlines and a rounded sans-serif for body text, both with a distinct thread structure and a faint shadow effect to reinforce depth.

title: Style 95
description: For journey-style handouts dividing a long process into four named stages.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal magazine/guide infographic on a white background with plenty of air and a large, clear main headline at the top in bold sans-serif. Layout and structure: Divide the content into 4 large sections (categories) arranged in a 2x2-like flow but connected by soft, winding rounded frames/lanes (like a colored path). Each section has a colored header strip with the section name and a small parenthetical line (e.g. a date range). Beneath each header: 3 circular medallions in a row, each with a mini-illustration in "travel poster" style. Beneath each circle: a short title (1 line) and a short description (1–2 lines). The section frames should be rounded, soft, and ideally have a subtle gradient matching the section's color theme. Add small decorative elements (e.g. leaves/flowers/icons) near some section corners to reinforce the guide feel. Illustration style: The mini-illustrations should be clean digital illustrations, semi-flat with soft gradients and light depth (not photorealistic). Bright colors, clear shapes, "travel poster" feel. Light drop shadow/outline around the circles for separation from the white background. Color palette: White background. Four color themes: cool blue/purple, green/pink, yellow/orange, red/brown. Harmonized pastel-saturated tones, no neon colors. Typography: Sans-serif throughout, clear hierarchy, and scannable text. Section headings clear, ideally in a rounded banner. Negative instructions: Avoid dark background, neon/HUD, vintage paper, grunge, photorealism, 3D render, overloaded text blocks, hard angular boxes.

title: Style 96
description: For workshop roadmaps and project-plan sketchnotes that feel facilitator-made.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "sketchnote roadmap" infographic that looks hand-drawn with a marker pen and light watercolor fill, like a workshop poster on white paper: warm white background with subtle paper texture, thin black hand-sketched contours, uneven lines, and small shadings that look quickly sketched by a facilitator. The composition should be a clear journey/roadmap: a thick, winding red path with a dashed center line that begins at a "START" sign in the lower left and leads in soft curves through several named sections to a concluding destination in the upper right; the path should act as the visual anchor that ties everything together. Place 5–6 large "SECTION" headlines in blue highlight strips (like highlighter marker) along the path, with short subheadings in black, and around each section there should be 1–3 yellow post-it-like fact boxes with rounded corners and a small shadow, filled with short microcopy; the post-it boxes should have small pin/tape illustrations and occasionally a small header line in bold. Add many small doodle icons and vignette illustrations in the same hand-drawn style: a construction crane and boxes, document stacks, microphone/podcast, brain/mindmap, maps and diagrams, shields/checklists, HR documents, social icons, speech bubbles, stars, and arrows; keep them simple, children's-book-like but professional, with flat color + light watercolor shading in muted tones (red, blue, yellow, green, gray). Include a large title box in the upper left inside a framed hand-drawn box with quotation marks and bold headline text, plus several thin arrows pointing from boxes to icons to create connections; also use small handwritten labels on banners and occasional "callout" clouds. Typography should look handwritten/marker-drawn but still readable: headlines in blocky uppercase, body text in simple handwriting, with consistent margins and clear hierarchy; use blue highlight bars for section titles and yellow for content boxes, plus red for the path and a few accent stars. The overall impression should be an energetic yet orderly whiteboard sketch/illustrated map: many objects but clear flow, playful doodles, post-it notes, and a red path guiding the eye; avoid photorealism, slick UI cards, neon/HUD, perfect vector-ruler lines, and minimalist corporate design — it should feel like a hand-drawn, pedagogical roadmap poster with marker pen and watercolor fill.

title: Style 97
description: For classical literature, mythology, or poetry projects styled as a painted story atlas.
prompt: Create a vertical, poster-like "illustrated story atlas" infographic in classic storybook/mythology style with a digitally painted gouache/oil-painting feel: soft brushstrokes, subtle grain, light paper texture, and a muted, cinematic color palette (deep navy blue, fog gray, sea green, pale moonlight, warm sand/ochre for typography). The composition should feel like a curated book plate: a large elegant title at the top in classic serif with high contrast, a thin decorative border/frieze under the title (antique/Greek feel), and then a large central scene that fills most of the surface (dramatic landscape with sky and sea, strong atmospheric depth, soft fog, and light gradient toward the horizon). In the sky there should be discreet star fields and several constellation diagrams drawn as thin bright lines between star points (precise, minimalist, like astronomy charts) with small serif labels beside them, plus a large painterly cloud mass creating drama (possibly with a subtle lightning flash/light flare) but without becoming photorealistic. Along the left edge a vertical row of 4–6 circular vignette medallions should be placed, each with a small narrative miniature scene in the same painterly style, framed by a thin ring and a short caption beneath in serif (like a "caption" in an illustrated guide). The main scene should also contain a small solitary "hero" figure/object in the middle/lower third (silhouette-like but clearly painted) to provide focus, and on the right side a dramatic figure or symbol in cloud/air should loom as a mythic allegory (stylized, not horror, more like classic illustration). Text layout should be sparse but clearly hierarchical: headlines in warm beige/ochre serif, body text in light warm-white serif with short paragraphs, and a few pointing texts placed directly on the image with discreet shadows for readability. The overall impression should be "premium vintage illustrated infographic": poetic, historical, museum-like, with calm tones, strongly narrative imagery, clean typographic blocks, and no modern UI elements, no neon colors, no flat vector icons, and no hard contours — just soft, hand-painted atlas/storybook aesthetic with constellation lines and circular vignette panels guiding the eye through the narrative.

title: Style 98
description: For exploration, trade-route, or migration lessons on an antique wall-chart.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "antique atlas wall chart" in classic National Geographic history-map style with a warm parchment background, light blotches/wear, a thin decorative frame, and a large world map in the middle in pale blue/cream tones with fine coastlines, old-fashioned typography, and dense geographic labels. On top of the map, many thin color-coded route lines (red, blue, green, brown) should cross oceans and continents as elegant arcs, with small dot markers and discreet arrows; the lines should feel like they've been printed on an old nautical chart (not neon, no thick strokes). Around the map, a large number of small oval portrait medallions with sepia/painted faces (vintage engraving/photograph feel) should lie scattered and be anchored to places with thin lines; each medallion should have a small name plate in serif and a short textual note in a small printed font below, like a museum catalog. Along the bottom edge, a wide timeline/legend panel should run the full width with many small boxes, years, icons, and short descriptions in dense editorial text, plus small object illustrations (compass, ships, artifacts) in engraving/watercolor style; everything should be strictly gridded and feel like a printed poster from a historical society. Typography should be old-fashioned and cartographic: headlines in elegant serif with small caps, body text in very small serif, and map labels in classic atlas style; use discreet ornaments such as compass roses and scale bars in the corners. The color palette should be muted and antique: parchment beige, pale sea blue, sepia for frames/medallions, and sparing accent colors in the routes; avoid modern UI elements, glassmorphism, hand-drawn sketchnote, and strong gradients — the goal is a print-ready, antique world map/infographic with portrait medallions, crossing expedition lines, decorative frame, and a dense historical timeline panel at the bottom.

title: Style 99
description: For art-nouveau art-history and salon-poetry handouts with aristocratic polish.
prompt: Tonality and mood: The aesthetic should radiate aristocratic elegance and nostalgic authority. The style is deeply inspired by Alphonse Mucha and combines an educational demeanor with timeless beauty. Visual identity — Background color: #F3EAD3 (warm, creamy parchment tone). Text color: #3E2F26 (dark, earthy brown). Accent color: #A83E36 (terracotta red). Secondary color palette: #D9B87F, #6D8EA6, #6E8B5E, #8F8376. Imagery and style — Graphic traits: Hand-drawn lithographs including ornamental banners, vignette frames, and detailed ink cross-hatching. Elements of historical portraits and classical compositions. Texture and surface: The surface should imitate aged paper with visible fibers, watercolor wash, and fine stippling. Composition and light: A segmented collage where decorative text scrolls tie the various illustrative zones together. Lighting is soft and diffuse (daylight) for scenic portions, while the layout's structure has a flat graphic illumination. Detail work — Line work: Thin black contours with fine cross-hatching technique. Coloring: Muted, desaturated watercolor tones held strictly within the ink lines. Ornamentation: Art Nouveau-inspired corner ornaments and curling banners in the spirit of Mucha. Typography — Headlines: Classic Victorian antiqua with heavy weight. Body text: Traditional antiqua in Garamond style, slightly condensed. Stylistics: Uppercase is used for headings inside banners; italic is used for emphasis in descriptions. Color strategy: Deep sepia for running text and terracotta red for primary titles to create a clear visual hierarchy.

title: Style 100
description: For myth-and-invention lessons where technical precision meets allegorical storytelling.
prompt: This design should radiate an authoritative and mythological aura that feels both instructive and timeless, where technical precision meets allegorical storytelling. The visual foundation rests on a papyrus-like background in the tone #DCCB96, where text in a deep brown shade (#2B2118) and a luminous turquoise accent color (#009DA0) create a clear contrast. To bring the composition to life, a secondary palette consisting of terracotta (#B55A2F), deep blue (#2B5D91), gold (#D4A034), and olive green (#6B8E23) is used. The imagery draws its power from classical frontalism with flat figures in profile, hieroglyphic borders, and isometric pyramids, where technical labels are integrated into a symbolic and architectural framework. The surface should imitate aged papyrus with rough, organic edges and a matte finish reminiscent of carved stone. The layout is built as a triptych with three clearly defined panels framed by geometric borders, where the size of the figures is determined by their hierarchical importance. Lighting is flat and uniform, entirely without drop shadows, which focuses attention on the clean dark-brown contour lines resembling ink on an ancient scroll. Technical concepts such as speed or components are translated visually through metaphors like sphinxes or massive stone blocks. For headlines, a stylized serif imitating cuneiform or stone inscriptions is used, preferably enclosed in cartouches or banners, while a clean, modern sans-serif ensures maximum readability in the explanatory texts.

title: Style 101
description: For museum-style themed exhibits — species, artefacts, or historical figures in niches.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal museum-style illustrated infographic poster with a classic, antique feel. The background should be a soft sky with bright clouds and a warm, sand-colored atmosphere. Layout and composition: At the top, a large centered serif main headline and a smaller subtitle beneath. Centrally, a symmetrical architectural frame (temple/columns/arcades) that looks like an exhibition hall in stone, with a headline plate/banner above the frame. In the frame: a row of 8–12 decorative niches/portals (vaults) with a stylized figure/icon per niche. Each niche has its own color-tinted background (muted blue, wine red, teal, green, etc.) and a short caption beneath. Bottom: a secondary band with smaller panels (3–6) containing small illustrated scenes/figures and short text blocks, framed in bright boxes. The whole poster should have a clear grid and symmetry, but the background (clouds/landscape) softly ties the whole together. Illustration style: Digitally painted 2D in "storybook/gouache" feel: soft shadows, rounded shapes, subtle texture, no hard black contours. The architecture should feel like carved stone with ornaments (friezes, small patterns, laurel wreaths). Figures should be stylized and clear, with a classic poster feel (not realistic portraits). Color palette: Base: warm sand/beige, ochre, terracotta, soft olive. Accents in niches: muted deep blue, wine red, teal, emerald, but harmonized and "antique". Light, warm overall without neon. Typography: Classic serif for headlines, small captions under each niche, legible and evenly placed. Finish: Feel of a curated exhibition poster: elegant, pedagogical, richly illustrated but well-ordered. Negative instructions: Avoid modern neon/tech, dark HUD style, photorealism, 3D render, grunge, coarse outlines, minimalist corporate look without ornament, chaotic collage layout.

title: Style 102
description: For Renaissance-invention history handouts with a nostalgic inventor's notebook feel.
prompt: This design should be infused with a historical and sophisticated tone that feels both authoritative and inventive with a nostalgic undercurrent. The visual identity rests on a base of antique white #DDC9A3 as the background color, where the primary text in a deep dark brown shade #2E1F14 is contrasted by a golden amber accent #C98E45. To create visual depth, a palette consisting of earthy tones such as #8B5E3C and #5A4632 is used, complemented by touches of deep red #9C3828 plus creamy and mustard yellow shades like #F4EBD9 and #E8B96D. The imagery focuses on mechanical typewriters, brass cogs, and pocket watches placed beside vintage photo frames and parchment scrolls with schematic arrows. The surface should have a distinct texture of aged paper with worn edges, the metallic sheen of brass, worn leather, and fine etching lines. The composition is built around a central mechanical main object from which connections branch out radially to framed subject areas, all anchored at the base by a large open book. The lighting creates a warm sepia feel with soft vignetting, where a focused amber glow illuminates digital details in otherwise diffuse ambient light. The illustration technique imitates classic copperplate engravings and ink washes, framed by ornamented wood borders, metal pipes, and flowing banners. Typography consists of a strong, condensed vintage-style headline (Woodtype Slab Serif), a classic antiqua in Garamond or Caslon style for body text, and uppercase condensed sans-serif for captions, while personal comments and signatures are rendered in an elegant cursive handwriting.

title: Style 103
description: For history and mythology lessons that demand a dramatic black-and-gold stage.
prompt: The aesthetic for this design should be sophisticated and authoritative, characterized by a sumptuous and dramatic demeanor with deeply symbolic content. The visual identity rests on a background in near-total blackness (#050507), where text in a creamy shade (#F2E6CF) is contrasted by a luxurious accent color in gold (#D4AF37), complemented by a secondary palette consisting of muted gold-brown (#8C7348), dark petrol blue (#2B3A42), neutral gray (#4A4A4A), and off-white (#FFFFF0). The imagery draws inspiration from Art Deco with intricate geometric borders, sunburst motifs, and metallic gradients, where the architectural contrast between a dark industrial factory on the left and a radiant skyscraper on the right functions as a visual metaphor for demand and evaluation respectively. The composition is a symmetrical three-part layout framed by heavy ornamentation with corner florets and diamond shapes, where a central symbolic diagram in the form of a pyramid structure is flanked by illustrative vignettes and a flow chart like a champagne tower. The surface should convey the feeling of polished gold leaf against a matte velvet background with an atmospheric glow and razor-sharp vector precision without grain. The lighting builds on cinematic chiaroscuro with shimmering gold highlights against deep shadows and a simulated backlight behind the central elements. The iconography is stylized with flat shading and metallic contour lines. Typographically, an elegant antiqua with high contrast in the style of Mincho or Didot is used for the dominant English-language headlines, which are given a metallic color gradient and bold weight, while a clean sans-serif in medium weight is used for readability in the diagrams, complemented by French subtitles in smaller uppercase.

title: Style 104
description: For launch posters, reading weeks, and themed events with tactile layered-papercut shine.
prompt: Create a vertical poster in "premium papercut/layered quilling" style with a clear 3D paper feel: deep navy space background with small sparkling stars, and around the edges large organic paper layers that look like cut-out waves at many levels (quilling rolls and contour lines) in saturated but soft colors (turquoise, teal, purple, magenta, coral, orange, yellow, mint), with clear shadows between each paper layer and a light fiber texture in the paper. Centrally, a large headline in uppercase should sit with an "embossed/foil" feel (warm gold tone, slightly raised, soft shadow) and beneath it a smaller subheading; typography should be clean sans-serif but feel embossed on paper. In the middle a small papercut-style rocket should float, leaving behind a long, flowing rainbow-colored "quilling" smoke/flame tail composed of dense paper strips and twisted spirals, with strong depth and shadows that make it feel handmade. Scatter several planets and stars that are also paper layers (circular, relief, with rings and topographic layer lines) in the corners and side edges, plus some small decorative star shapes that look like die-cut paper stars. Add a section with 3–5 short "key points" placed as small text blocks with icon pictograms beside them (simple papercut icons such as document, globe, gear, brain, speech bubble), where the icons also have layers and shadows; keep the text short, scannable, and evenly placed without becoming a rigid UI grid. Use soft spotlight glows and vignette to draw focus to the headline and the rocket, but keep everything as physical paper (no neon HUD effects, no photorealistic materials like metal or glass beyond subtle "foil" on the headline). The overall impression should be a highly detailed, craft-like papercut space poster with quilling details, deep shading, gold-embossed headline, rainbow smoke from the rocket, and colorful layered planets along the edges, like an elegant, playful premium infographic.

title: Style 105
description: For high-prestige launches — new textbooks, annual school reports, or flagship CPD courses.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "premium dark product promo" graphic in Amazon/KDP marketing style with dramatic studio lighting and a luxurious gold theme. The background should be a dark, textured surface (charcoal/brown-gray stone or concrete) with subtle grain, soft vignetting, and a warm spotlight from upper left that creates glow and reflections on the floor. On the left, a 3D-rendered book/mockup should stand slightly angled (front visible, slight perspective depth), with a high-contrast cover design in black and metallic gold: large title in embossed/foil gold typography with glitter/sparkling particles, and a hero illustration of gold bars or gold fragments glowing, ideally with a prop hinting at "gold rush" (e.g. a pickaxe/tool) in the same gold shine; add subtle rim light along the book edges and a soft shadow on the surface for realism. On the right, a vertical list of 5–6 bullet points should sit, where each point has a clear checkmark icon in gold (rounded badge or simple check in a circle) and a short line of text in warm white/light beige sans-serif, with certain keywords highlighted in gold to match the theme; keep spacing and alignment consistent so it feels like a professional advertisement. Add a thin decorative line or divider between certain bullets, and place a small gold line icon in the lower right (e.g. a stylized emblem, coin, or simple symbol) for balance. The color palette should be strict: black/charcoal, warm brown-gray, warm-white text, and metallic gold as the primary accent; use glow/bloom very sparingly and only around the gold, letting the rest stay matte. Typography should be modern and salesy: large gold-foil display, clean sans-serif, high legibility, no hand-drawn elements, and no neon colors; the overall impression should be a high-converting premium product ad with gold feel, cinematic lighting, and a luxurious "gold rush" aesthetic.

title: Style 106
description: For engineering and architecture lessons rendered as deep-blue technical blueprints.
prompt: Overarching Design Guidelines Tonality: The aesthetic should be precise, structural, and industrial with a pedagogical and authoritative feel. Visual Identity Background color: #1E5DA2 (Deep technical blue) Text color: #FFFFFF (Chalk white) Accent color: #A8D5FF (Light blue highlight) Complementary color palette: #0F3666, #5A8CC9, #DCEEFF Imagery and Execution Character: White line art depicting technical drawings, isometric projections, and schematic floor plans. Include measurement scales, dashed indicator lines, and a discreet grid background. Texture and surface: The surface should have a matte finish with a subtle overlay of grid paper. Use consistent vector lines for a clean and professional impression. Composition and light: Modular layout divided by vertical dashed lines. Balanced split-screen for comparisons, framed by technical borders and header blocks. Flat diagrammatic lighting without directional shadows to ensure the highest possible readability and contrast. Technical details: Line weight: Thin, uniform white strokes (approx. 1px–2px). Diagram type: Hybrid between a 2D floor plan (left) and an isometric 3D model (right). Decorative elements: Measurement annotations, arrows, dotted connector lines, and technical control stamps. Typography Headings: Geometric sans-serif (e.g., DIN, Roboto, or Noto Sans JP). Hierarchy: Bold headings in uppercase, smaller technical labels, and translations inside brackets. Style: Functional labeling that is easy to read against the dark background. Alignment: Centered main titles and left-aligned descriptions.

title: Style 107
description: For technical subject briefs where white line-drawings on blue carry the authority.
prompt: This design setup should radiate an industrial and pedagogical feel with clearly authoritative and structural precision. The graphic profile rests on a deep blue background in color code #1E5DA2, where all text is kept in pure white (#FFFFFF) and complemented by a light blue accent color (#A8D5FF) plus a palette composed of the shades #0F3666, #5A8CC9, and #DCEEFF. The imagery is defined by technical drawings rendered in white line art, including isometric projections, schematic floor plans, and precise measurement lines with dashed indicators set against a discreet grid background. The surface should have a matte finish and the feel of grid paper with consistent vector lines of uniform thickness around 1px to 2px. The composition is modular and divided by vertical dashed lines in a balanced split-screen layout, where the left side shows a two-dimensional floor plan and the right side a three-dimensional isometric stack, all framed by technical borders and header blocks. The lighting is entirely flat and diagrammatic with no directional shadows to ensure maximum contrast and readability. Decorative details such as arrow markers, dotted connector lines, and technical stamps reinforce the engineering impression. Typographically, a geometric sans-serif similar to DIN or Roboto is used, where main titles are centered in bold uppercase while smaller technical labels and explanations inside brackets are left-aligned to maintain a clean and functional hierarchy against the dark background.

title: Style 108
description: For STEM lessons that use CAD-style gears and flow-charts to show how systems work.
prompt: This design vision is characterized by an analytical and authoritative tone with a focus on a precise, structural, and pedagogical presentation. The visual core rests on a deep blue background (#1C4E80) combined with razor-sharp white text (#FFFFFF) and a distinct red accent color (#E85D5D), while secondary elements in yellow (#F2D64B) and light blue (#8FBCE6) create visual balance. The imagery draws inspiration from the CAD world and uses mechanical gear motifs, flowcharts, and modular information blocks placed on top of a subtle paper texture with a white linear grid. The composition is built as a logical comparison between problem and solution in a two-part layout, framed by technical borders that include scale markers and measurement bars. The lighting is consistently flat and high-contrast to prioritize clear lines over gradients, and for emphasis, coarse yellow brushstrokes with a chalk-like texture are used. At the bottom of the layout sits a CAD-style footer containing metadata for scale, date, and author. Typographically, a powerful geometric sans-serif is used for main headings and a clean, monolineal sans-serif for body text, where a hierarchy of heavy, medium, and regular weight is applied with left-aligned text blocks and centered diagrams.

title: Style 109
description: For edtech product guides styled as an isometric premium tech handbook.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "editorial isometric explainer infographic" in modern NotebookLM/product-guide style with a light warm-white background, subtle paper grain, and a clean grid feel; use a mix of isometric 3D-ish illustrations and flat UI elements that feel like a premium tech manual. The composition should have a large title at the top left in dark blue-gray serif/sans-serif (printed editorial), and the main area should be dominated by a large isometric "machine/metaphor" scene in the middle (e.g., a small system of rooms/buildings/workshop) drawn in soft semi-3D with thin outlines, light shadows, and muted material colors (wood/warm brown, glass/blue-gray, metal/gray), where clear pipes/channels tie the parts together and show flow. Place three clear step markers (1–3) around the scene with short headings in bold sans-serif, and use arrows/lines to guide the eye from step 1 to step 3; to the left of the scene there should be a vertical list of "inputs" represented as small flat icon boxes with simple pictograms (document, webpage, video) connected into the pipe system. On the right there should be a separate dark blue side panel (a "blueprint card") that takes up the full height: it should have white lines, small diagram mockups, headings in white sans-serif, and several sections stacked vertically with thin separators, like a technical manual; within this panel there should be small wireframe boxes, icon examples, and short instruction texts in microcopy style, all very clean and readable. Stylistically, the illustrations should feel like a mix of architectural visualization and infographic: isometric shapes, perspective lines, intersection shadows, subtle highlights on glass, and small decorative details such as plants, small screens, and a camera/projector-like unit, but without photorealism. The color palette should be controlled and professional: warm-white background, blue-gray lines, navy accent in the side panel and headings, plus small accent colors in orange and teal for buttons/markers; avoid neon, hand-drawn sketch, and heavy grunge. The typography should have a clear hierarchy: large title, short step headers, and small labels; the overall impression should be a well-structured, modern, print-friendly explainer with an isometric main metaphor, pipe flows, and a right column that looks like a blueprint instruction panel.

title: Style 110
description: For analytical data briefs where a clean technical palette keeps attention on the numbers.
prompt: This design profile is characterized by an analytical and pedagogical feel that emphasizes precision, structure, and professionalism in every detail. The visual framework rests on a very light gray, near-white backdrop (#F9F9F9) where the main text in dark anthracite gray (#222222) is contrasted by a bright blue accent color (#5B9BD5), while touches of deep red (#C0392B), pale blue (#EAF4FC), neutral gray (#888888), white (#FFFFFF), and light gray (#D3D3D3) are used to clarify the graphic hierarchy. The visual expression is built on technical line art with isometric renderings, architectural floor plan symbols, precise measurement annotations, and logical directional arrows. The surface should mimic smooth digital paper with clean vector lines and schematic clarity entirely free of color gradients, supported by flat and uniform lighting that guarantees maximum visibility for all elements. The composition consists of a comparative split-screen layout with a vertical flow that follows a strict grid, where generous white space creates harmonious balance between the technical diagrams. The line work consists of thin and precise strokes of 1–2 pixels for the load-bearing structures, while logical flows are marked with dashed lines. To guide the viewer, blue fills are used for active components, while red dashed arrows indicate critical transitions or anomalies. Typographically, a powerful and bold sans-serif in gothic style or Helvetica is used for the main headings, while the body text is set in a legible sans-serif of medium weight. For technical specifications and smaller annotations, a condensed sans-serif or fixed-width typeface is used, where the visual order is governed by clear size differences between main headings, section dividers, and explanatory body copy.

title: Style 111
description: For design-tech, invention-history, or patent-themed units rendered as pen-and-ink blueprints.
prompt: Create a horizontal technical blueprint/patent poster with a warm off-white "aged paper" background and subtle paper texture. The style should be pen-and-ink technical line art: thin, precise lines, cross-hatching, and light stippling for shadows. Layout & composition: Place a thin frame around the entire poster and include discreet ruler/measurement markings along the outer edges (like a drafting template). At the top: a large heading in bold sans-serif, uppercase, centered. Beneath the heading: a smaller subtitle in classic serif. In the middle: a single large hero diagram illustration extending horizontally and showing a gradual transformation between two visual systems (organic → mechanical), but without locking it to any specific topic. Around the hero illustration: measurement lines, arrows, dashed guide lines, and labels in small caps, with figure references such as "(Fig. 1.2a)," etc. Below the hero illustration: a clear figure caption in the form "[short description]." At the bottom: a discreet footer bar that feels like a book/poster. At the bottom right: a small stamped information box (volume/series/classification) in the same line-art style. Color & print feel: Keep everything nearly monochrome (black/gray lines) but use two muted accent colors to distinguish two parts of the hero diagram (e.g., muted burgundy for the left part and muted steel blue for the right part). No gradients, no glow, no strong colors—it should feel like a printed drawing poster. Typography: Heading: bold sans, large caps. Subtitle and figure text: serif, academic. Labels: small, technical, consistent spacing and alignment. Negative instructions: Avoid photorealism, 3D render, neon/HUD, modern app icons, pastel illustrations, large color fields, soft gradients, cartoon style, handwritten text.

title: Style 112
description: For high-energy evening events, after-school clubs, and disco-themed posters.
prompt: This design should convey an energetic and visually stimulating feel that is both modern and educational, inspired by a nightlife-infused retro manner. The visual identity is grounded in a very dark background color (#0D0A12) with white text (#FFFFFF) and an intensely pink accent color (#FF2A68), supported by a palette consisting of cyan (#00F0FF), amber yellow (#FF9F00), purple (#9D00FF), and midnight blue (#1B143F). The visual style is distinguished by simulated neon tubes and directional arrows placed in rounded containers, along with iconographic metaphors such as brains and cocktail glasses. Contrasts are created by pitting a coarse surface of dark brick against the smooth and luminous glass tubes. The composition is built around a hierarchical flow with a centered heading and a balanced comparison between a left and right side. The lighting is dominated by self-illuminating neon sources with powerful halo and bloom effects that cast colored ambient light on the textured background, where the lines have a uniform style with rounded caps. The typography consists of a retro-inspired script for headings, like luminous tubes, while the body text is set in a rounded sans-serif. To perfect the style, the text should have a white core with an outer colored glow that harmonizes with the color of the surrounding container.

title: Style 113
description: For future-tech and AI lessons with urgent neon holographic authority.
prompt: This design should be infused with a technical and analytical tone with an urgent futuristic feel built on high visual contrast. The visual identity rests on a dark blue, near-black base (#050512) with white text (#FFFFFF) and an intense cyan accent color (#00FFFF), which is complemented by a vibrant palette consisting of magenta (#FF00FF), yellow (#FFFF00), indigo (#4B0082), and bright blue (#0055FF). The visual style is distinguished by holographic wireframes, icons of brains and gears with neon glow, and code snippets and glitch artifacts layered over a hexagonal grid. The texture combines digital noise and CRT scan lines with glass-like interface panels set against a blurred, rain-soaked urban environment glimpsed deep in the background. The composition centers on a logical flow chart with great depth, where interconnected nodes and floating text boxes in semi-transparent blue-tinted panels with beveled corners create order in the information flow. The lighting is built on self-illuminating neon emissions against deep shadows to mimic a backlit screen, while arrows and connectors are designed as both jagged lightning bolts and straight luminous vectors. Typographically, a bold heading in geometric sans-serif with neon glow is used, while the body text is set in a clean fixed-width typeface or DIN style to ensure technical precision and good readability by using semi-transparent backgrounds behind the text boxes.

title: Style 114
description: For AI-in-school staff briefings that want a high-end futuristic HUD entrance.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal futuristic tech/AI infographic in "dark mode neon HUD" style. The background should be charcoal black with a subtle gradient and light noise. Use high contrast and glowing neon light. Composition & structure: Large main heading at the top, centered, in clean technical sans-serif (white or light gray). Divide the content into 3–4 steps with small headings "STEP 1," "STEP 2," etc. above each cluster. Left section: show a dense data matrix/column visual (thousands of small horizontal bars or dots in cyan/teal on black) that looks like raw data. Center section: an exploded layer stack in isometric 3D: 6–8 floating plates with semi-transparent "glass" surface, neon edges, glow, and dot/particle patterns. Each layer has a short label on the left in a vertical list. Right section: a glowing 3D cube/volume in neon colors and a stylized neural network (nodes and lines) with faint cyan glow. Insert arrows/chevrons between the sections to show flow from left → center → right. In a corner at the bottom: a small legend/panel in HUD style (framed box, neon lines) with 2–4 color-coded rows. Style & rendering: Isometric/3D vector, "glassmorphism," semi-transparent surfaces, clear neon outlines. Outer glow/bloom around key shapes, small light particles in the layers, thin technical lines. Color palette: Black/dark gray base. Neon accent in cyan/teal, electric blue, magenta/purple, and lime green. Keep everything "sci-fi conference slide": clean, technical, high polish. Negative: No paper texture, no vintage, no hand-drawn style, no soft pastels, no photorealism. Extra "negative" lines (if the model strays): Avoid skeuomorphism, warm beige tones, serif typefaces, coarse grunge, low contrast, flat 2D pictograms without glow.

title: Style 115
description: For upper-secondary economics, business, or data-analysis units with a terminal-screen look.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "futuristic finance dashboard" infographic in dark neon/tech style (cyber UI) with a deep dark blue/black background, subtle light rays, glowing lines, and a faint grid/bokeh that creates the feel of a digital market terminal. At the top, a large, centered heading in clean sans-serif uppercase should sit on a dark header bar, with a date/period label on the right in a light blue tone; the typography should be modern, crisp, and high-contrast (white/light gray for text). The main layout should consist of 5 equally wide vertical "glassmorphism" cards side by side with rounded corners, neon outlines, and soft inner shadows, where each card has its own accent color theme (blue, cyan, green, orange/gold, purple) that recurs in icon, arrow, and graph. In each card: place a small logo-like icon in a rounded badge at the top, followed by a short title row and a smaller label row; in the middle, a large numeric value in bold sans-serif should dominate, below it a clear trend indicator (up/down arrow) in matching neon color with a small percentage label; at the bottom, a small line graph/sparkline should sit in a dark panel with a glowing line and light gradient fill beneath the curve. Below the graph there should be a short "trend" section in smaller text with 2–3 lines, separated by thin divider lines, so the layout feels like a professional product card. Use consistent UI detailing: thin separators, small labels in uppercase, microtext, badges, and soft glow effects around the cards' edges; add discreet light reflections/highlights in corners that mimic glass. The color palette should be neon but controlled: cyan/teal, electric blue, lime green, amber/orange, and neon purple against a dark background, with bloom/outer glow but without becoming overexposed. Avoid vintage, paper texture, hand-drawn style, flat pastels, and photorealism; everything should feel like a modern digital finance panel/YouTube thumbnail of a market dashboard with strong structure, high polish, neon glow, and clearly card-based imagery.

title: Style 116
description: For computer-science lessons told across a glowing circuit-board cityscape.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal neon-cyber "HUD explainer infographic" in dark sci-fi UI style with a deep black/dark blue background and a dense circuit board pattern of luminous traces in cyan, green, and orange that creates the feel of a digital city/circuit-board wall. At the top, a large, centered title should sit in a futuristic bold sans-serif with neon-green accent and a framed header plate with glowing edges. The layout should be clearly divided into a left vs. right theme with a large central metaphor in the middle: a neon-lit intersection/Y-split road radiating in a cyan→purple→orange gradient and drawing the eye into the image; the road should have glowing edge lines, small light particles, and perspective depth like a synthwave highway. On the left side, two large UI panels (glassmorphism cards) should be stacked vertically with rounded corners, dark semi-transparent background, neon frames in blue/cyan, and small icon badges; each panel contains a large illustration in sticker-like semi-flat 3D with glow (e.g., an AI/robot/diagram) and short headings in white text with neon accent, plus small geometric symbols and numbers as "data confetti." On the right side, two similar panels should be stacked vertically but with a warmer accent color (orange/amber) and clear warning markers (triangle, alert badge, red stamp), including a panel that looks like a "news card" or "warning window" with glitch lines and a large red "FALSE" stamp on the diagonal. At the far right, a separate "CALL TO ACTION" box should sit as a large neon-framed card with a clear heading and a large main phrase in uppercase, like a poster in UI form; the frame should be green/cyan with strong glow and inner shadow. Use many UI details: small buttons, window tabs, badge labels, pixel glow, thin separators, and soft bloom around all edges; the text should be scannable and high-contrast (white/light gray) with accent words in neon colors. The color palette should be strictly cyber: cyan/teal, neon green, purple/magenta, and orange/amber on a dark background, with controlled bloom and no photorealistic elements; avoid hand-drawn sketch, vintage, flat pastels, and minimalist corporate design—the goal is a dramatic, highly polished sci-fi dashboard infographic with a neon road in the middle, two columns of glowing UI panels, and a clear call-to-action box.

title: Style 117
description: For AI-literacy and digital-skills briefs with glassy isometric polish.
prompt: This design vision is permeated by a futuristic and sophisticated tonality that evokes an analytical sense of high-tech precision. The visual base rests on a deep, space-like background in the shade #020B1C, where the ice-blue text (#E1F5FE) and the intense cyan-blue accent color (#00E5FF) create sharp contrast against a palette of secondary tones in magenta (#FF4081), deep blue (#2979FF), electric turquoise (#18FFFF), and darker navy (#102035). The imagery focuses on isometric circuit boards, glowing data pathways, and holographic interfaces interwoven with technical schematics and visualized integrated circuits. The textures alternate between self-illuminating glass and polished metal resting on a digital grid with soft color transitions. The composition is symmetrically built around a central circular flow where isometric nodes are linked via logical paths in a flow-chart structure with distinct zones. The lighting is characterized by neon glow, inner luminescence, and dramatic bloom effects against a dark void, where the perspective creates the illusion of three-dimensional objects on a two-dimensional plane. The lines are thin and precise, like luminous traces on a circuit board, and framed by tech-inspired borders with subtle corner details. For headings, a powerful geometric sans-serif is used in uppercase and bold, such as Orbitron or Eurostile, while the body text is kept in a clean modern sans-serif or monospace. The typographic hierarchy is razor-sharp with clear size differentiation and color-coded emphasis, where main labels are written in uppercase and descriptions in regular sentence form to maintain technical authority.

title: Style 118
description: For video-feedback and social-media-literacy lessons rendered as a neon UI wireframe.
prompt: Create a dark, futuristic "neon-outline explainer graphic" in 16:9 with a deep navy/black gradient background and subtle vignette. All elements should be line illustrations with soft neon glow and gradient stroke (cyan→blue→purple/pink), like a clean wireframe UI sketch: a large central rectangle that looks like a video player (play icon, progress bar, small button), below/beside it a long rectangular "comment box" with an icon (e.g., rosette/medal or speech bubble) and short text in white/cyan sans-serif; on the left, a small mobile/phone icon with several small "PROMPT" tags popping out like speech bubbles. Connect these blocks with clear flow logic: a large curved arrow from the video player down to the comment box, and a dotted arrow/line from the phone icon into the comment box; also add a small label/tag on a diagonal "tag" (hanging label) with neon stroke. The typography should be modern, uppercase sans-serif for headings and smaller sans for body text, all in light cyan/white with minimal shadow and high contrast against the background; use generous air and even margins. Add a large magnifying-glass icon partially over the comment box to create focus, and keep all edges rounded and consistent. No photorealism, no textures apart from faint noise/grain in the background; no filled color blocks—only neon outlines, glow, dotted lines, arrows, and UI icons in a clean, pedagogical "tech tutorial overlay" style.

title: Style 119
description: For AI and machine-learning CPD slides where floating wireframe brains set the tone.
prompt: Atmosphere and tone: A sophisticated and analytical profile with a visionary feel that functions pedagogically and instructively. Visual profile Base color: #020712 (Dark navy blue). Main text color: #E1F5FE (Light ice blue). Accent color: #00E5FF (Electric cyan). Support colors: #FF5252, #2979FF, #D1C4E9, #18FFFF. Graphic elements Floating isometric screens and models of brains in wireframe format. Luminous particle streams and hexagonal data blocks. Interfaces in semi-transparent glass (glassmorphism).

title: Style 120
description: For fast-paced upper-secondary tech units with urgent holographic energy.
prompt: Feel and character: A technological and analytical aesthetic with a sense of urgency, future, and sharp contrasts. Visual Profile Background color: #050512 (Dark blue blackness). Main text color: #FFFFFF (Pure white). Accent color: #00FFFF (Clear cyan). Complementary colors: #FF00FF, #FFFF00, #4B0082, and #0055FF. Graphic Components Holographic wireframes along with symbolism around brains and gears. Glowing directional arrows and excerpts of code sequences. Visual glitch effects and overlays with hexagonal grids.

title: Style 121
description: For single-slide tip cards and AI-tool quick-reference with a glassy app feel.
prompt: Create a minimalist "glassy purple UI tip card" in 16:9 with a dark background and soft purple gradient haze (deep violet→indigo) that flows diagonally across the image; add subtle film grain and faint bokeh dots so it feels like a premium app presentation. Center three horizontal, pill-shaped buttons (rounded capsules) stacked with even spacing; each pill should have a semi-transparent glass look, soft inner glow, and a thin neon edge in purple, with light drop shadow and faint bloom around the edges. To the left of each text pill there should be a small square icon button with rounded corners, also glassy with neon edge, containing a simple monochrome UI icon (e.g., group/plus, expand arrows, speech bubble), in white/lavender with minimal glow. The text in the pills should be large, clean sans-serif in white with a light purple tint and subtle soft shadow, left-aligned within each pill and with good padding; keep the rest of the surface completely clean without extra elements. Use a clear hierarchy: the top pill slightly lighter, the middle normal, the bottom with a subtle highlight on the last word (e.g., through a slightly lighter gradient on that part of the text) but without colorful marking. No photorealism, no hard lines, no illustrations—only an elegant, modern "app UI overlay" with purple glassmorphism, soft neon glow, three capsule rows, and small icon boxes on a dark, hazy background.

title: Style 122
description: For fusion-themed comparison slides — old vs new tools, teaching methods, or paradigms.
prompt: Create an infographic with "high-energy sci-fi fusion" imagery: dark black/blue background with massive, cinematic light glow and showers of sparks; composition in two clear halves meeting in the middle with a large "+" sign in white/yellow glow; the left side dominated by warm orange/gold plasma energy, the right side by cool cyan/blue electric energy, both with powerful particles, embers, light rays, and a strong central lens flare near the bottom edge; place two large, rounded "app tile" boxes in the middle (one on the left, one on the right) with thick neon outline, inner glow, and glassy/metallic surface, appearing to float above a glossy, dark "wet asphalt/metal" surface that reflects light and colors; create a clear energy arc/energy-ring effect around each tile (orange on the left, blue on the right) with glowing edge, small star particles, and electric cracks; typography: a single massive main heading at the top "AUTOMATION" in extra bold sans-serif, 3D/emboss feel, warm orange-to-yellow gradient, strong glow and subtle "heat haze"/motion streaks behind the text; the design should feel like an action-packed tech cinematic poster, extreme contrast, lots of bloom/inner glow, crisp edges, clear center alignment, minimal small text, no people, no platform UI, no timestamps, no watermarks, focus on dramatic energy, sparks, neon frames, and reflective surface.

title: Style 123
description: For digitalisation-transformation stories shown as a clear before-to-after journey.
prompt: Create a high-contrast infographic with a neon-cyber/tech aesthetic and a clear before→after narrative. Composition: divide the scene into left and right halves with a strong, glowing arrow/connector pointing from left to right (thick arrow, neon purple/cyan edge glow, light motion blur, and particles along the edge). Left side: "messy/old" feel with stacks of books/paper bundles/notes in semi-3D (stylized but detailed), darker blue/purple background with sparks and light streaks. Right side: "premium/new" feel with an iconic, shining product/object in 3D (e.g., a safe, gift box, or trophy-like container) glowing from within with warm gold/orange glow; use strong rim light, mirrored metal edges, and flying gold particles/coins/sparkles around the object for "value" signal. Background: cosmic/energetic nebula in blue–purple with cyan highlights, radial light bursts behind the main objects, star particles, lens flare, and streaks for speed. Typography: extremely large, blocky sans-serif with clear hierarchy (top row in white, main word in cyan/blue gradient with strong outer glow, bottom row in yellow/gold with black stroke or deep shadow); maximum 6–10 words total, all caps, centered over the respective part but with the main word over the middle for maximum punch. Insert small UI-like badges/labels that stand out (rounded rectangles with neon glow) near the arrow for a "tech" feel. Color palette: cyan + electric blue + neon purple as primary, gold/orange as premium accent; hard contrast, powerful bloom, clear glows, sharp edges on text. Style rules: no flat 2D, no hand-drawn lines; everything should feel like modern "neon 3D thumbnail art" with exaggerated readability, dramatic lighting, high saturation, and energetic particles—optimized for visibility at small sizes.

title: Style 124
description: For youth-culture and hip-hop lessons with raw stencil-and-spray energy.
prompt: Atmosphere and feel: The aesthetic should radiate a rebellious and energetic force with a raw, authoritative urban character. Visual Identity Background shade: #3e3a36 (Dark, industrial gray-brown) Main text color: #ff6d00 (Strong, glowing orange) Accent color: #00d8ff (Luminous neon blue) Secondary tones: #76ff03, #651fff, #ffeb3b, #1a1a1a, #d50000 Imagery and Execution Motifs and elements: A backdrop of a naturalistic brick wall adorned with colorful spray-painted letters. Stencil-style iconography depicting gears, clockworks, and atoms. Graphic elements of dripping paint splashes and industrial piping. Surface and structure: A surface marked by rough brick texture, flaking plaster, and glossy paint details against raw concrete surfaces with natural defects. Composition and light: A centered vertical hierarchy with a dominant, massive heading block framed by architectural wall elements. Dramatic street lighting from above that creates sharp light reflections on the text and deep shadows beneath the protruding relief elements. Technical style: Medium: Digital graffiti simulation. Line quality: Heavy black outlines with lively and varying width. Depth: Text with clear, simulated 3D relief. Typography Heading: Custom "Bubble Graffiti." Body text: Sharp marker or brush script. Weight: Massive weight (Heavy/Black). Effects: Outer outlines, tonal fills, shadow effects, and realistic drip effects.

title: Style 125
description: For retro-comic mashup posters when a lesson wants pastel nostalgia with a punch.
prompt: This design should radiate an energetic and playful retro feel that is both nostalgic and instructive in its presentation. The visual base rests on a mint green background (#9ED9CC) that meets a soft pink tone (#F6C0C7) through a vertical division, where deep black text (#111111) and a red accent color (#E84E45) create clear contrasts along with secondary touches of white, gray, yellow, and cyan blue. The visual style is inspired by classic comic books with colorful character illustrations, speech bubbles, and checkered racing borders that frame the composition. The layout is bilaterally symmetrical and divided down the middle by a vertical chrome bar, with heavy heading fields at the top and bottom and central flow diagrams tied together by shiny metal arrows. The textures alternate between a matte paper finish with subtle halftone patterns and high-gloss metal effects, supported by flat illustrative lighting with neon-like glow and sharp light reflections on the chrome surfaces. Among the recurring motifs are gears, hamburgers, milkshakes, and diamonds that give a unique character to the whole. Typographically, a massive retro display style with white outlines, drop shadows, and 3D effects is used for headings, while the body text is kept in a clean and functional gothic sans-serif inside speech bubbles or rectangular text blocks to ensure the highest readability.

title: Style 126
description: For reading-week and cafeteria-theme posters with mid-century pop-culture warmth.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal retro infographic in 1950s "mid-century pop culture poster" style with a clear print feel: warm beige, lightly stained paper background with subtle grain, halftone/print texture, and faint color fading at the edges. Use playful, hand-lettered/comic display typography for the main heading (thick letters with dark outline and light shadow) and place it in a large, rounded banner at the top with pastel gradient (turquoise/teal) and a contrasting secondary banner below in pink, both with "chrome"-like highlight lines and thick outlines that feel like 1950s sign painting. The main layout should consist of three large, equally wide cards/panels side by side (left–middle–right), each panel with heavily rounded corners, multilayered frames (mint/teal/pink), and a retro "inset" feel with inner shadow, like plastic/chrome fittings. Inside each panel there should be a large central icon/illustration in clean 2D retro-cartoon style with soft gradients and clear outlines (sticker feel), plus short text blocks in bullet form with small pictograms/emoji-like icons as bullets; keep the text scannable and well-aligned with clear headings in bold retro display style. Insert small decorative "atomic age" ornaments around in the background: starbursts, boomerang shapes, small sparks, and confetti dots in pastel (mint, light yellow, coral, light blue), plus some small sign-like detail (e.g., neon badge) in the same style. The color palette should be consistently 1950s: turquoise/teal, mint green, soft pink, coral red, butter yellow, and muted black for outlines; use subtle highlights that mimic chrome and plastic, but avoid photorealism and 3D render. The overall impression should be a nostalgic, cheerful poster infographic with clear frames, central illustrations, retro typography, atomic age decorations, and printed paper finish; avoid modern minimalist corporate style, neon HUD, thin line diagrams, and overly clean vector lines without texture.

title: Style 127
description: For cybersecurity, cryptography, or programming units with authentic terminal aesthetics.
prompt: This design is characterized by a logical, systemic, and technical tonality with a digitally authoritative feel. The visual identity rests on a background of absolute blackness (#000000), where the text in light gray (#F0F0F0) is sharply contrasted against an intensely neon-green accent color (#00FF41). To create a complex graphic interface, magenta (#D633B3) is also used for code highlighting, dark forest green (#1A331A) for muted surfaces, plus details in amber yellow (#FFB86C) and pure white (#FFFFFF). The visual style is based on a central terminal window with a rectilinear vertical list structure framed by borders resembling circuit boards and technical-mechanical outlines with circuit nodes. The content includes line numbering, syntax highlighting, and HUD elements, all presented with clean vector lines that have a subtle feel of CRT scan lines and a matte digital finish. The surface is covered by a layer of digital noise, and in the background a faint rain of binary code or a geometric grid can be glimpsed. The light is self-illuminating and high-contrast, where the neon-colored text seems to glow against the black void. The typography consists of pixelated monospace typefaces in console style for headings and fixed-width typefaces for code in the body text, where keywords are highlighted in magenta or pink while comments are kept in a muted white tone.

title: Style 128
description: For hacker-club, CTF-event, and retro computing lessons with Matrix-style atmosphere.
prompt: This design profile is permeated by a technically authoritative and nostalgic feel with a cryptic hacker spirit, where the visual identity rests on a bottomless blackness in color code #050505. The dominant text color in terminal green (#2CFF56) is complemented by an amber yellow accent (#FFB200) along with secondary tones in dark green (#00551A), white (#FFFFFF), and dark gray (#1E1E1E). The visual style combines ASCII art and pixel graphics with circuit board patterns in wireframe model, data streams of binary rain, and technical flow charts in a classic command prompt interface. To reinforce the retro feel, textures such as phosphor glow from old CRT screens, scan lines, digital noise, and high-contrast pixelation are used. The layout is modularly built vertically with dashed dividers and a central main graphic depicting a computer or terminal, surrounded by clearly defined zones for headings, comments, and diagrams. The lighting is built on self-illuminating neon elements that seem to glow against the dark void to simulate screen light, where the line work consists of thin, often dotted lines of a single pixel width. The framing is done with double lines or terminal-style brackets, while the margins are adorned with hex codes, binary strings, and ASCII elements. Typographically, pixelated block headings are used in uppercase and a fixed-width typeface (monospaced) for body text, where color-coded syntax highlighting is used to create clear visual emphasis.

title: Style 129
description: For programming-history and vintage-computing units rendered as a CRT terminal.
prompt: Overall tone: Cryptic, technical, bleak, nostalgic, and with a high-tech command-line interface.
