Graphic Profile: Background shade: "#020502" Character color: "#4AF626" Accent shade: "#1A3B1A" Complementary colors: ["#0D1F0D", "#88FF88", "#002200"] Imagery and Style: Hallmarks: Vintage CRT screen hardware, flowing Matrix-like code, terminal interfaces, pixelated interference Surface and structure: Thick scan lines, phosphor bloom, grain, screen surface curvature, chromatic aberration. Composition: Centered focus on the monitor, immersive small perspective shift, claustrophobic framing focused on the digital readout. Lighting: Muted ambient darkness, main illumination via screen radiation (green phosphor glow), volumetric light effects from the screen. Screen effect: CRT bloom and interlaced lines Mood: Dark, underground hacker lair Typography: Heading: Monospace Pixelated (e.g., VT323, Fixedsys) Body text: Monospace terminal font Font weights: Regular, Bold for commands Character set: Alphanumeric mixed with Katakana/Kanji

title: Style 130
description: For call-to-action posters — safety campaigns, attendance drives, or whole-school initiatives.
prompt: This design vision should radiate an urgent and commanding tonality with a motivating, industrial, and authoritative character reminiscent of powerful graphic manifestos. The visual framework rests on a warm cream-colored background (#F6EEDA) where the deep black text (#0D0D0D) and an intensely red accent color (#CC2A22) create dominant contrast, complemented by secondary tones in charcoal black (#1F1F1F), muted beige (#E8DCC5), and dark red (#A6211A). The visual style is defined by powerful silhouettes and industrial iconography such as gears, factories, megaphones, and hammers, combined with dynamic motion lines and coarse directional arrows. The surface should have a tactile feel of matte vintage paper with screen-printed grain and digital noise that completely lacks gloss. The composition is governed by a dynamic diagonal layout divided by heavy dividers and characterized by high visual density. The lighting is strictly flat and graphic without any color gradients, where high-contrast two-tone shading is used to create sharpness. The line work is executed with heavy and uniform strokes framed by a powerful black border, while the symbolism is often metaphorical, such as broken chains or watchful eyes. Typographically, an extremely heavy and condensed sans-serif or Japanese gothic in "Extra Bold" weight is used, where the text is either block-aligned or placed at dramatic angles with solid fills and simple shadow effects to reinforce the authoritative message.

title: Style 131
description: For workshop and safety briefings in sloyd, woodwork, or chemistry with industrial warning energy.
prompt: This design vision should radiate a robust and authoritative power that feels both urgent and pedagogical through a tangibly tactile expression. The visual identity rests on a neutral light gray background (#EBEBEB) where the deep black text (#121212) and a sharp warning-yellow accent color (#FFC20E) create clear readability, supported by a palette in dark gray (#2B2B2B), muted blue (#5B85AA), and earthy tones in ochre and brown. The imagery is characterized by industrial elements such as diagonal warning stripes, bolted metal frames, and heavy machinery combined with gear metaphors, technical blueprints, and speech bubbles for instructions. The surfaces should have a raw feel of weathered concrete, scratched metal, and a layer of grunge noise with touches of oil stains and worn paper to reinforce the mechanical theme. The composition is strictly modular and organized as a grid of sign-like panels with thick frames adorned with rivets, where the information is divided into three clearly numbered main sections. The lighting is flat but uses textured shading and hard contrasts on the mechanical details to give them physical weight, all executed with thick and raw ink outlines mimicking technical sketches. Hand-drawn industrial symbols such as wrenches and safety helmets complement the whole, while the typography is built on a massive industrial gothic sans-serif for headings and a functional sans-serif in medium weight for body text, giving an expression reminiscent of stencil-painted warning signs in a factory environment.

title: Style 132
description: For hardware-focused tech lessons showing real components paired with schematic diagrams.
prompt: This design vision should radiate a sense of technical authority and industrial precision through a marked focus on hardware and robust reliability. The visual base rests on a background in a neutral, light gray metal tone (#cfd3d6) with text in deep black (#1f1f1f) and a bright yellow accent color (#ffc107) that acts as a guide through the technical details. The imagery integrates photorealistic hardware components with two-dimensional circuit diagrams on a substrate of brushed aluminum and scratched metal, where the textures alternate between rubber insulation on cables, matte plastic, and authentic industrial grime. The composition centers on an information plate that appears to be bolted onto a worn workbench, framed by workshop instruments such as digital calipers, screwdrivers, and coiled cables that together create a natural border. Inside this field, yellow circuit lines are used as guide rails to tie together various modular sections and direct the viewer's gaze between hardware renderings and text blocks. The lighting mimics the cold and diffuse ceiling light of a workshop, creating soft, realistic drop shadows beneath the 3D objects, while subtle glow effects from yellow LED indicators bring the surface to life. Engraved panels and riveted sheet metal reinforce the physical presence and feel of durable construction. Typographically, a massive industrial sans-serif in uppercase is used for main headings, like DIN Condensed or Impact, while the body text is kept in a clean and utilitarian typeface such as Roboto or Helvetica. The text appears either stamped with dark ink directly onto the metal or deeply etched into the surface, where bold headings in frames clearly mark the technical specifications to maintain a strict hierarchy.

title: Style 133
description: For earth-science and astronomy units where a planet cutaway carries the whole lesson.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "premium National Geographic-style space-to-Earth cutaway infographic" with photorealistic, cinematic lighting and extremely high detail. The background should be deep space black with a starfield and a large view of Earth's curvature running diagonally across the image; let the atmosphere have a thin blue glow/aurora band along the horizon. Integrate a large, realistic land and sea surface with clouds, coastlines, and topography, and build in a dramatic geological cutaway in the foreground: a cut-out "wedge" of the Earth's crust in perspective revealing layers (crust, mantle) with glowing orange/red inner channels, cracks, and magma flows, along with a smoking volcano on the surface; use realistic materials and soft shadows so the cutaway feels like a physical 3D model. Place several large numbered callouts (1–5) in airy, modern sans-serif with a light blue/white number, positioned over different parts of the scene, and for each number a short heading and 2–3 lines of text in small white sans-serif; keep the text in discreet blocks with plenty of air and precise alignment. On the left side, a large, vertical main heading should sit in thin, elegant sans-serif (very large, all caps, with wide line spacing) with a subheading and a short introductory text beneath, like a magazine feature; use light blue/white typography that contrasts with the space background. Add thin, semi-transparent arrows and flow lines across sea/land and into the cutaway (white/gray) that show circulation/transport, plus small labels directly on the geological layers in very discreet text (light gray) that follow the surfaces. Include a small inset of the moon or a small diagram figure near the top (photorealistic moon disk and a minimal line diagram), with thin leader lines. The overall impression should be a high-budget documentary infographic: photorealistic space+Earth view, 3D geological cross-section model with glowing interior, airy numbered storytelling text, thin annotation lines, and a sober color palette (blue atmosphere, white text, orange magma), without neon HUD, without hand-drawn sketch, and without flat vector elements.

title: Style 134
description: For art-history and expressionism units with textured impasto energy.
prompt: This design should be permeated by a dynamic and artistic tonality that feels conceptual, energy-filled, and profound. The base is formed by a deep, purple-black background tone with color code #1a1025, where white text (#FFFFFF) and an intensely yellow accent color (#FFD600) create lively contrasts against a secondary palette consisting of red (#D32F2F), blue (#1976D2), black (#121212), and light gray (#E0E0E0). The visual style is characterized by an abstract expressionist expression with a powerful impasto texture, where thickly applied oil paint and raw brushstrokes meet the visible structure of a canvas, complemented by dynamic paint splashes and subtle code snippets embedded in the paint layers. The composition is built on a three-part narrative structure resting on top of a centered abstract swirl, where dark, semi-transparent gradients and vignetting are used strategically to lift the white text from the chaotic background. The artistic technique mimics a combination of acrylic pouring and palette knife work, with a layer of grunge providing extra depth. The lighting creates an inner luminescence in the painting itself, while the text layers are presented with flat and clear lighting. Typographically, large, hand-painted numbers are used in a heavy calligraphic brush style with organic edges, while the headings are set in a bold, modern sans-serif in gothic style with powerful drop shadows. The body text is kept in a clean, white sans-serif in medium weight, where all text elements are given an outer glow or shadow effect to ensure they stand out against the detail-rich and noisy backdrop.

title: Style 135
description: For steampunk-themed creative-writing and invention-history projects with retro-future flair.
prompt: This design should convey a feel of adventure and technical fantasy with an industrial and authoritative tone reminiscent of a bygone future. The visual identity is anchored in a deep blue background evoking the infinity of space (#0d1b33), where a central illustration of a steampunk astronaut stands in focus against a backdrop of glowing nebulae. The composition is built as a symmetrical collage with a central motif surrounded by radial information points, connected through complex conduits of copper pipes and intermeshed brass gears (#cd7f32). The surfaces should have a tactile and worn feel of weathered paper and brushed bronze, where analog gauges and skeuomorphic industrial details such as rivets and pipes create technical depth. The lighting is cinematic with a warm rim light on the main figure that contrasts with the cold surrounding starlight, while the metallic surfaces are given sharp reflections and the paper fields cast soft shadows to reinforce the layer-on-layer effect. The framing consists of torn paper edges and riveted metal frames, and the iconography consists of clean line icons embossed on round copper medallions. Typographically, a worn slab-serif in vintage style is used for headings, while the body text is kept in a clean, condensed, and geometric sans-serif. To maximize readability and contrast, the dark brown text (#2b1d0e) is placed on fields of light beige parchment, where large and powerful headings are clearly separated from the more densely packed informative body copy.

title: Style 136
description: For workflow briefs showing a tool-to-result journey with neon-flow momentum.
prompt: Create an infographic with a modern "tech-neon" look: dark background with deep gradient (navy→purple→teal) and subtle light rays/particles/bokeh that add energy; clear two-part composition left→right showing "from tool to result" with a strong center arrow/chevron pointing right, the arrow should have neon glow (turquoise/green) and function as visual flow; the left side should feel like UI/tool screens with floating cards/panels in glassy "glassmorphism" (transparent boxes, soft corners, thin lines, blue/purple glow, drop shadow), the right side should feel like a finished published result in a browser window/mockup (clean white surface but with warm highlight and a clear "PUBLISHED" badge/stamp with strong contrast and glow); place large, thick heading rows at the bottom that dominate (bold condensed sans-serif, uppercase, extremely high readability), use two-color typography with gradient or color alternation per row (cyan/teal for some words, warm yellow/orange for others) plus clear outline/outer glow to pop against the background; include a small "callout" label near the arrow (e.g., a rounded rectangle with neon edge and text) for extra "hook," but keep other elements few and clean; use strong lighting with edge glow around main objects, clear depth feel via shadows and perspective, light vignette that draws the eye toward the middle, and a "premium digital" finish (crisp, high-contrast, saturated but controlled), no watermarks, no timestamps, no platform UI elements.

title: Style 137
description: For AI-tool collection posters — a tile-grid showcase of the classroom's go-to apps.
prompt: Create an infographic with a "dark tech + neon ember" aesthetic: very dark background (almost black/anthracite) with subtle gradients and light vignette, high contrast and premium digital finish; in the center a grid of rounded "UI cards/tiles" (3x3 feel) with thin neon edges, soft inner glow, and drop shadow, each tile contains a simple icon in glowing orange/gold (like warm neon/fire), preferably with a light 3D/emboss feel and small particles/sparks around the icons; at the top a large, clean title/logo style in white/light gray sans-serif with soft glow and plenty of air (large typographic hierarchy), preferably with a color accent in the logo as a subtle gradient but keep the rest sober; at the bottom a massive "hook" text that takes up a lot of space (extra bold, rounded/condensed sans-serif, uppercase), white fill with thick black outline and additional orange glow/gradient accent on one word to create punch, the text should sit as a "banner" without covering the main motif too much; the whole should feel like a futuristic dashboard/screen with glassy highlights, reflections, and light bloom around neon, but with clear readability on mobile (large shapes, few elements, strong separation); color palette: black/anthracite + warm orange/gold as primary glow, with small touches of neutral gray/white for text; no timestamps, no platform UI, no watermarks, no excessively many details, focus on clear thumbnail composition and "premium tech course/overview" feel.

title: Style 138
description: For astronomy and data-visualisation units told in minimalist silken orbit ribbons.
prompt: Create a wide, horizontal "data-art astronomy infographic" in ultra-minimal dark mode with a deep black background and extremely clean, thin lines. The main imagery should be long, elegant, multicolored trace curves flowing across the entire canvas like silk ribbons: thousands of densely layered lines in a warm spectral gradient (red→orange→yellow→green→cyan) forming large concentric swirls/spirals around small planet bodies, with soft transitions and no glow—only clean printed precision. Use large circular "orbit halos" and rings as thin gray/blue lines around certain planets, plus several soft, sweeping paths that cross the image and tie the swirls together in a cohesive flow composition; let the lines create a clear left-to-right movement. Place photorealistic or very subtly rendered planets as small, crisp spheres on black (with correct lighting and light shadow), but keep them small so the line work dominates. Place small, extremely discreet labels in white/light gray microtypography (sans-serif) near swirls and paths, with thin leader lines and dotted segments; also include a small legend with color dots and short text in minimal style. Include an "asteroid/comet" column as a row of small gray stone silhouettes or miniature images placed vertically with small labels. At the bottom, a narrow timeline/scale strip should run across the width on a dark blue tinted base: many tick marks, small years, and a few marked points with small green/blue labels, all in very fine line weight. The composition should feel like an award-winning information graphic where negative space and line rhythm are the main aesthetic: no boxes, no large color fields, no hand-drawn style, no glassmorphism, and no neon HUD; only black background, thin precise lines, spectral trace bands, small planets, microtext annotations, and a bottom-edge timeline in strictly minimalist data visualization style.

title: Style 139
description: For literature, design-history, and cross-cultural units pairing Sumi-e brushwork with isometric precision.
prompt: This design vision unites timeless elegance with an authoritative feel through a sophisticated cultural fusion where East Asian tradition meets modern engineering. The visual foundation rests on a background of aged and fibrous rice paper (#F4F1E8), where deep black text (#0D0D0D) and a dark red accent color (#8A1C15) create harmonious contrast. The imagery is characterized by a unique combination of classical ink paintings (Sumi-e) and razor-sharp, modern isometric diagrams, where expressive brushstrokes with varying thickness are used to frame and structure the content. The surface is marked by tactile details such as ink bleed, subtle splash effects, and dry brushstrokes that give an organic feel to the technical presentations. The composition is bilaterally symmetrical and divided by a central vertical axis, resulting in a balanced two-column layout where delicate nature sketches of trees or temples are set against precise 3D renderings of products. The lighting is flat and ambient, true to the analog paper medium, where depth is created through the varying density of the ink and layered shading rather than through photorealistic light. The red accent color is used sparingly as a focal point and divider, like the traditional seal stamps found on antique scrolls. Typographically, a classic antiqua is used, for example Garamond or Caslon, where the hierarchy is governed through bold weight for main headings and underlined section headers with decorative flourishes, which guarantees the highest readability against the textured background.

title: Style 140
description: For exploded-view engineering briefs where a central object breaks into labelled components.
prompt: This design vision is characterized by an analytical and authoritative feel where engineering and technical sharpness take center stage. The visual framework rests on a midnight blue backdrop (#0b1623) that supports light gray text (#f0f0f0) and a distinct orange accent color (#ff9f30). The imagery focuses on a technical exploded view in wireframe format, where a central object is shown in its individual components against a discreet geometric drafting grid. The composition is governed by angled leader lines and modular information clusters that create a clear data structure around the central motif. The surface should be perceived as a digital drawing with razor-sharp vector lines in white and orange, illuminated by flat but high-contrast light that gives the lines an almost self-illuminating quality against the matte, schematic surface. Typographically, a condensed sans-serif in uppercase is used for main headings, where the orange color is used to highlight titles and labels while the white color is reserved for the descriptive body copy. By combining bold weight in the headings with clean and functional body text, a sophisticated and easily navigable technical hierarchy is created that underscores the precision of the document.
